zh: 史籍载述，五千年前近东一带已有人掌握书写之术，是为人类记事之嚆矢。
en: We can read of things that happened 5,000 years ago in the Near East,where people first learned to write.

zh: 然世界仍有若干地域，迄今未尝有文字传世。
en: But there are some parts of the world where even now people cannot write.

zh: 彼辈欲存其史，唯赖口传之萨迦——即一代代说书人递相授受之英雄传说。
en: The only way that they can preserve their history is to recount it as sagas—legends handed down from one generation of storytelers to another.

zh: 此类传说自有其价值，可略示远古部族迁徙之迹，惟当时无人能执笔以录其行事。
en: These legends are useful because they can tell us something about migrations of people who lived 5 long ago,but none could write down what they did.

zh: 人类学家想知道现在生活在太平洋岛屿上的波利尼西亚人的远祖来自哪里。
en: Anthropologists wondered where the remote ancestors of the Polynesian peoples now living in the Pacific Islands came from.

zh: 这些人的传说表明，他们中的一些人来自大约 2000 年前的印度尼西亚。
en: The sagas of these people explain that some of them came from Indonesia about 2,000 years ago.

zh: 但是，第一批像我们一样的人生活在很久以前，甚至连他们的传奇故事（如果有的话）也被遗忘了。
en: But the first people who were like ourselves lived so long ago that even their sagas,if they had any,are forgotten.

zh: 因此考古学家既没有历史也没有传说来帮助他们找出第一批“现代人”的来源。
en: So archaeologists have neither history nor legends to help them to find out where the first ‘modern men'came from.

zh: 然而幸运的是，古人用石头制造工具，特别是燧石，因为它比其他种类更容易成型。
en: Fortunately,however,ancient men made tools of stone,especially flint,because this is easier to shape than other kinds.

zh: 他们也可能使用过木头和兽皮，但这些都已经腐烂了。
en: They may also have used wood and skins,but these have rotted away.

zh: 石头不会腐烂，因此很久以前的工具仍然存在，即使制造它们的人的骨头也消失得无影无踪。
en: Stone does not decay,and so the tools of long ago have remained when even the bones of the men who made them have disappeared without trace.

zh: 罗宾·普莱斯《寻觅化石人》插图：出土于约克郡锡默斯沼之磨光石斧 第一单元 第一课
en: RoBIN PLACE Finding fossil man 三 Polished axeheads found at Seamers Moor in Yorkshire Unit 1 Lesson 1

zh: 您可能想知道，为什么蜘蛛应该成为我们的朋友？
en: Why,you may wonder,should spiders be our friends?

zh: 因为它们消灭了如此多的昆虫，而昆虫中包括一些人类最大的敌人。
en: Because they destroy so many insects,and insects include some of the greatest enemies of the human race.

zh: 昆虫将使我们无法在这个世界上生存；如果没有食虫动物的保护，它们会吞噬我们所有的庄稼，杀死我们的牛羊。
en: Insects would make it impossible for us to live in the world;they woulddevour all our crops and kill our flocks and herds, if it were not for the protection we get from insect-eating animals.

zh: 我们固然要感谢那些捕食昆虫的飞禽走兽，但若将其所杀昆虫悉数合计，其数目仍远不及蜘蛛所消杀者。
en: We owe a lot to the birds and beasts who eat insects but all of them put 5 together kill only a fraction of the number destroyed by spiders.

zh: 此外，与其他一些食虫动物不同，蜘蛛永远不会对我们或我们的财物造成最小的伤害。
en: Moreover,unlike some of the other insect eaters,spiders never do the least harm tous or our belongings.

zh: 正如许多人认为的那样，蜘蛛不是昆虫，甚至与昆虫也没有近亲关系。
en: Spiders are not insects,as many people think,nor even nearly related to them.

zh: 略加审视即可分辨：蜘蛛恒具八足，昆虫之足则至多不过六。曾有人在英格兰南部草地上估算，每英亩蜘蛛逾二百二十五万只；若以此推之，一块足球场大小的地面上，不同种类蜘蛛总数约可达六百万之众。
en: One can tell the difference almost at a glance,for a spider always has eight legs and an insect never more than six. 10 spiders in a grass field in the south of England,and he estimated that there were more than 2,250,000 in one acre;that is something like 6,C00,000 spiders of different kinds on a football pitch.

zh: 蜘蛛至少有半年时间忙于捕杀昆虫。
en: Spiders are busy for at least half the year in killing insects.

zh: 我们无法对它们杀死的数量做出最疯狂的猜测，但它们是饥饿的生物，不满足于一日三餐。
en: It is impossible to make more than the wildest guess at how many they kill,but they are hungry creatures,not content with only three meals a day.

zh: 据估计，英国一年内被蜘蛛消灭的所有昆虫的重量将超过该国所有人类的总重量。 10ck 102 T。
en: It has been estimated that the weight of all the insects destroyed by spiders in Britain in one year would be greater than the total weight of 15 all the human beings in the country. 10ck 102 T.

zh: GILLESPIE 饶恕《倾听者》中的那只蜘蛛 一只蜘蛛消灭了一只蚱蜢 第 1 单元第 2 课
en: GILLESPIE Spare that spider from The Listener A spider destroys a grasshopper Unit 1 Lesson 2

zh: 现代登山运动员试图沿着一条能给他们带来良好运动的路线来登山，而且越困难，就越受重视。
en: Modern alpinists try to climb mountains by a route which will give them good sport,and the more difficult it is,the more highly it is regarded.

zh: 然而，在开拓时期，情况根本不是这样。
en: In the pioneering days,however,this was not the case at all.

zh: 早期的登山者正在寻找最简单的登顶方法，因为登顶是他们所寻求的奖品，尤其是如果以前从未到达过。
en: The early climbers were looking for the easiest way to the top,because the summit was the prize they sought, especially if it had never been attained before.

zh: 确实，在他们的探索过程中，他们经常面临最危险的困难和危险，其装备方式会让现代登山者一想到就会不寒而栗，但他们并没有特意去如此兴奋。
en: It is true that during their explorations they often faced 5 difficulties and dangers of the most perilous nature,equipped in a manner which would make a modern climber shudder at the thought,but they did not go out of their way to uch excitement.

zh: 他们只有一个目标，一个唯一的目标——登顶！
en: They had a single aim,a solitary goal—the top!

zh: 现在我们很难体会到先驱者们的艰辛。
en: It is hard for us to realize nowadays how difficult it was for the pioneers.

zh: 除了采尔马特和夏蒙尼等一两个地方迅速流行起来外，阿尔卑斯山的村庄大多是被高山与文明隔绝的贫困聚居地。
en: Except for one or two places such as Zermatt and Chamonix,which had rapidly become popular,Alpine villages tended to be impov- 10 erished settlements cut off from civilization by the high mountains.

zh: 这样的旅馆一般都很脏，跳蚤猖獗；食物只是当地的奶酪，配上通常十二个月的面包，全部用粗酒冲下。
en: Such inns as there were were generally dirty and flea-ridde n;the food simply local cheese accompanied by bread pften twelve months old,all washed down with coarse wine.

zh: 通常，一个山谷根本没有旅馆，登山者会尽可能地找到庇护所——有时是当地的牧师（他通常和他的教区居民一样贫穷），有时是牧羊人或奶酪制造商。
en: Often a valley boasted no inn at all,and climbers found shelter wherever they could—sometimes with the local priest(who was usually as poor as his parishioners),sometimes with shepherds or cheese-makers.

zh: 背景总是一样的：肮脏、贫穷，而且非常不舒服。
en: Invariably the background was the same:dirt and poverty,and very uncom- fortable.

zh: 最近，俄罗斯报道了几起案例，人们可以用手指阅读和检测颜色，甚至可以透过坚固的门和墙壁看到东西。
en: Several cases have been reported in Russia recently of people who can read and detect colours with their fingers,and even see through solid doors and walls.

zh: 其中一个案例涉及一名 11 岁的女学生维拉·佩特洛娃 (Vera Petrova)，她视力正常，但也能用皮肤的不同部位以及透过坚固的墙壁感知事物。
en: One case concerns an eleven-year-old schoolgirl,Vera Petrova,who has normal vision but who can also perceive things with different parts of her skin,and through solid walls.

zh: 这种能力首先被她的父亲注意到。
en: This ability was first noticed by her father.

zh: 有一天，她走进他的办公室，碰巧把手放在了锁着的保险箱的门上。
en: One day she came into his office and happened to put 5 her hands on the door of a locked safe.

zh: 突然，她问父亲为什么把这么多旧报纸锁在那里，甚至还描述了它们是如何捆扎起来的。
en: Suddenly she asked her father why he kept so many old newspapers locked away there,and even described the way they were done up in bundles.

zh: 维拉的好奇天赋引起了她居住地附近乌里扬诺夫斯克镇一家科学研究机构的注意，四月份，俄罗斯联邦共和国卫生部的一个特别委员会对她进行了一系列测试。
en: Vera's curious talent was brought to the notice of a scientific research institute in the town of Ulyanovsk, near where she lives,and in April she was given a series oftests by a special commission of the Ministry of Health of the Russian Federal Republic.

zh: 在这些测试中，她能够通过不透明的屏幕阅读报纸，更奇怪的是，通过将肘部移到儿童的乐透游戏上，她能够描述印刷在上面的数字和颜色；并且，在另一个例子中，穿着丝袜和拖鞋，用她的脚辨认出隐藏在地毯下的图片的轮廓和颜色。
en: During these tests she was able to read a newspaper through an 10 opaque screen and,stranger still,by moving her elbow over a child's game of Lotto she was able to describe the figures and colours printed on it;and,in another instance,wearing stockings and slippers,to make out with her foot the outlines and colours of a picture hidden under a carpet.

zh: 其他实验表明她的膝盖和肩膀也有类似的敏感性。
en: Other experiments showed that her knees and shoulders had a similar sensitivity.

zh: 在所有这些测试中，维拉都被蒙住眼睛；事实上，除了蒙住眼睛的时候，她缺乏用皮肤感知事物的能力。
en: During all these tests Vera was blindfold;and,indeed,except when blindfold she lacked the ability to perceive things with her skin.

zh: 研究还发现，虽然她可以用手指感知事物，但当她的手湿了时，这种能力就消失了。
en: It was also found that although she 15 could perceive things with her fingers this ability ceased the moment her hands were wet.

zh: 埃里克·德·莫尼 (ERIC DE MAUNY) 看到《倾听者》的双手 另一位俄罗斯女孩罗莎·库勒绍娃 (Rosa Kuleshova) 蒙着眼睛在朗读。
en: ERIC DE MAUNY Seeing hands from The Listener Another Russian girl,Rosa Kuleshova,reads blindfold.

zh: 第一单元第 4 课
en: Unit 1 Lesson 4

zh: 人们总是在谈论“青年问题”。
en: People are always talking about 'the problem of youth'.

zh: 如果有的话——我对此表示怀疑——那么创造它的就是老年人，而不是年轻人自己。
en: If there is one—which I take leave to doubt—then it is older people who create it,not the young themselves.

zh: 让我们脚踏实地，承认年轻人毕竟也是人——就像他们的长辈一样。
en: Let us get down to fundamentals and agree that the young are afterall human beings—people just like their elders.

zh: 老人和年轻人之间只有一个区别：年轻人前面有一个光辉的未来，而老人后面有一个辉煌的未来：也许这就是摩擦所在。
en: There is only one difference between an old man and a young one:the young man has a glorious future before him and the old one has a splendid future 5 behind him:and maybe that is where the rub is.

zh: 我发现年轻人令人兴奋。
en: I find young people exciting.

zh: 他们有一种自由的气息，他们没有沉闷的承诺，没有卑鄙的野心，也没有贪图安逸的感觉。
en: They have an air of freedom,and they have not a dreary commitment to mean ambitions or love of comfort.

zh: 他们不是焦虑的社会攀登者，也不热衷于物质事物。
en: They are not anxious social climbers,and they have no devotion to material things.

zh: 在我看来，这一切都将它们与生命和事物的起源联系起来。
en: All this seems to me to link them with life,and the origins of things.

zh: 从某种意义上说，他们似乎是宇宙生物，与我们郊区的生物形成了暴力而可爱的对比。
en: It's as if they were,in some sense,cosmic beings in violent and lovely contrast with us suburban creatures.

zh: 当我遇到一个年轻人时，所有这些都在我的脑海里。
en: All that is in my mind when I meet a young person.

zh: 他可能自负、无礼、自以为是或愚蠢，但我不会用尊重长辈的陈词滥调来寻求保护——就好像年龄本身就是尊重的理由一样。
en: He may be conceited,ill-mannered,presumptuous or fatuous,but I do not turn 15 for protection to dreary clichés about respect for elders—as if mere age were a reason for respect.

zh: 我承认我们是平等的，如果我认为他错了，我会作为平等的人与他争论。
en: I accept that we are equals,and I will argue with him,as an equal,if I think he is wrong.

zh: 菲尔登·休斯 (Fielden Hughes) 出自《空中》、《倾听者》 一群年轻人 第 1 单元第 5 课
en: FIELDEN HUGHES from Out of the Air,The Listener A group of young people Unit 1 Lesson 5

zh: 当我听到人们说体育在国家之间创造了善意，如果世界上的普通民众能够在足球或板球比赛中相遇，他们就不会愿意在战场上相遇，我总是感到惊讶。
en: I am always amazed when I hear people saying that sport creates goodwill between the nations,and that if only the common peoples of the world could meet one another at football or cricket,they would have no inclination to meet on the battlefield.

zh: 即使人们不从具体例子（例如1936年奥运会）知道国际体育竞赛会导致仇恨狂欢，人们也可以从一般原则中推断出这一点。
en: Even if one didn't know from concrete examples(the 1936 Olympic Games,for instance)that international sporting contests lead to orgies of hatred,one could deduce it from general principles.

zh: 当今几乎所有的运动都是竞技性的。
en: Nearly all the sports practised nowadays are competitive.

zh: 你玩游戏就是为了赢，除非你竭尽全力去赢，否则游戏就没有什么意义。
en: You play to win,and the game has little mean- ing unless you do your utmost to win.

zh: 在乡村绿地里，你站在一边，不涉及当地的爱国主义感情，你可以纯粹为了乐趣和锻炼而打球：但一旦出现声望问题，一旦你觉得如果输了，你和一些更大的单位就会蒙羞，最野蛮的好斗本能就会被唤起。
en: On the village green,where you pick up sides and no feeling of local patriotism is involved,it is possible to play simply for the fun and exercise:but as soon as the question of prestige arises,as soon as you feel that you and some larger unit will be disgraced if you lose,the most savage combative instincts are aroused.

zh: 任何参加过学校足球比赛的人都知道这一点。
en: Anyone who has played even in a school football match knows this.

zh: 在国际层面，体育运动坦白说就是模仿战争。
en: At the international level,sport is frankly mimic warfare.

zh: 但重要的不是运动员的行为，而是观众的态度：以及在观众背后，那些对这些荒谬的比赛感到愤怒的国家，他们认真地相信——至少在短时间内——跑、跳和踢球是对民族美德的考验。
en: But the significant thing is not the behaviour of the players but the attitude of the spectators:and,behind the spectators,of the nations who work themselves into furies over these absurd contests,and seriously believe—at any rate for short periods—that running, jumping and kicking a ball are tests of national virtue.

zh: 乔治·奥威尔 体育精神
en: GEORGE ORWELL The sporting spirit

zh: 并非动物发出的所有声音都可以用作语言，我们只需求助于蝙蝠回声定位的非凡发现，就能看到声音发挥严格功利作用的情况。
en: Not all sounds made by animals serve as language,and we have only to turn to that extraordinary discovery of echo-location in bats to see a case in which the voice plays a strictly utilitarian role.

zh: 为了充分理解这意味着什么，我们必须首先回顾一些人类最近的发明。
en: To get a full appreciation of what this means we must turn first to some recent human inventions.

zh: 大家都知道，如果他在墙壁或山腰附近喊叫，就会有回声回来。
en: Everyone knows that if he shouts in the vicinity of a wallor a mountainside,an echo willcome back.

zh: 距离固体障碍物越远，回声返回所需的时间就越长。
en: The 5 further off this solid obstruction,the longer time will elapse for the return of the echo.

zh: 敲击船体发出的声音会从海底反射出来，通过测量敲击与收到回声之间的时间间隔，就可以计算出该点的海水深度。
en: A sound made by tapping on the hull of a ship will be reflected from the sea bottom,and by measuring the time interval between the taps and the receipt of the echoes,the depth of the sea at that point can be calculated.

zh: 于是回声测深仪诞生了，现在在船舶上普遍使用。
en: So was born the echo-sounding apparatus,now in general use in ships.

zh: 每个固体物体都会反射声音，声音根据物体的大小和性质而变化。
en: Every solid object will reflect a sound, varying according to the size and nature of the object.

zh: 一群鱼就能做到这一点。
en: A shoal of fish will do this.

zh: 因此，从定位海底到定位鱼群是一个相对简单的步骤。
en: So it is a comparatively 10 simple step from locating the sea bottom to locating a shoal of fish.

zh: 凭借经验和改进的设备，现在不仅可以定位浅滩，还可以通过回声模式判断它是否是鲱鱼、鳕鱼或其他知名鱼类。
en: With experience,and with improved apparatus,it is now possible not only to locate a shoal but to tellif it is herring,cod,or other well-known fish,by the pattern of its echo.

zh: 研究发现，某些蝙蝠会发出吱吱声，通过接收回声，它们可以定位并避开障碍物，或者定位它们所吃的飞行昆虫。
en: It has been found that certain bats emit squeaks and by receiving the echoes,they can locate and steer clear of obstacles—or locate flying insects on which they feed.

zh: 蝙蝠的这种回声定位经常与雷达进行比较，其原理相似。
en: This echo-location in bats is often compared 15 with radar,the principle of which is similar.

zh: 莫里斯·伯顿 (MAURICE BURTON) 对动物生命的好奇 蝙蝠的回声定位通常与雷达进行比较。
en: MAURICE BuRTON Curiosities of animal life Echo-location in bats is often compared with radar.

zh: 第一单元第 7 课
en: Unit 1 Lesson 7

zh: 布鲁塞尔官员称，在美国屠宰的鸡不适合登上欧洲餐桌。
en: Chickens slaughtered in the United States,claim officials in Brussels,are not fit to grace European tables.

zh: 不，美国人说：我们的家禽很好，我们只是用不同的方式清洁它们。
en: No,say the Americans:our fowl are fine,we simply clean them in a different way.

zh: 如今，让富裕国家之间的贸易车轮陷入困境的，是国家法规的差异，而不仅仅是关税。
en: These days,it is differences in national regulations,far more than tariffs,that put sand in the wheels of trade between rich countries.

zh: 抱怨的不仅仅是农民。
en: It is not just farmers who are complaining.

zh: 一款符合欧盟安全标准的电动剃须刀必须经过美国测试人员的批准才能在美国销售，而美国制造的透析机则需要获得欧盟的批准才能进入欧洲市场。
en: An electric razor that meets the European Union's safety standards 5 must be approved by American testers before it can be sold in the United States,and an American-made dialysis machine needs the EU's okay before it hits the market in Europe.

zh: 事实上，一把在欧洲安全的剃须刀不太可能让美国人触电身亡。
en: As it happens,a razor that is safe in Europe is unlikely to electrocute Americans.

zh: 那么，问问大西洋两岸的企业，为什么要进行两次本来可以进行的测试呢？
en: So,ask businesses on both sides of the Atlantic,why have two lots of tests where one would do?

zh: 政治家们原则上同意，因此美国和欧盟一直在努力达成一项协议，以消除对许多产品进行双重测试的需要。
en: Politicians agree,in principle,so America and the EU have been trying to reach a deal which would eliminate the need to double-test many 10 products.

zh: 他们希望能在 5 月 28 日举行的美国和欧盟贸易峰会前及时结束。
en: They hope to finish in time for a trade summit between America and the EU on May 28th.

zh: 尽管谈判代表很乐观，但细节足够复杂，他们可能很难达成协议。
en: Although negotiators are optimistic,the details are complex enough that they may be hard-pressed to get a deal at all.

zh: 一个困难是制定协议。
en: One difficulty is to construct the agreements.

zh: 美国人很乐意就医疗设备标准达成一项协议，然后敲定涵盖电子产品和药品制造等领域的不同协议。
en: The Americans would happily reach one accord on standards for medical devices and then hammer out different pacts covering,say,electronic goods and drug manufacturing.

zh: 遵循欧洲大陆优良传统的欧盟希望就一般原则达成一致，这些原则可以适用于多种类型的产品，或许还可以扩展到其他国家。
en: The EU—following fine continental traditions—wants agreement on general principles, which could be applied to many types of products and perhaps extended to other countries.

en: From:The Economist,May 24th,1997 An electric razor that meets the European Union's safety standards must be approved by American testers before it can be sold in the United States.

zh: 阿尔弗雷德大帝充当自己的间谍，伪装成吟游诗人访问丹麦营地。
en: Alfred the Great acted as his own spy,visiting Danish camps disguised as a minstrel.

zh: 那时，流浪的吟游诗人到处都受到欢迎。
en: In those days wandering minstrels were welcome everywhere.

zh: 他们不是战士，他们的竖琴就是他们的护照。
en: They were not fighting men,and their harp was their passport.

zh: 阿尔弗雷德年轻时就学过他们的许多歌谣，并且可以通过杂技技巧和简单的魔术来改变他的节目。
en: Alfred had learned many of their ballads in his youth,and could vary his programme with acrobatic tricks and simple conjuring.

zh: 当阿尔弗雷德的小军队慢慢开始在阿瑟尔尼集结时，国王亲自出发渗透丹麦入侵者指挥官古斯鲁姆的营地。
en: While Alfred's little army slowly began to gather at Athelney,the king himself set out to penetrate the camp of Guthrum,the commander of the Danish invaders.

zh: 他们在奇彭纳姆定居过冬：阿尔弗雷德去了那里。
en: These had settled down for the winter at Chippenham:thither Alfred went.

zh: 他立刻注意到纪律松懈：丹麦人有着征服者般的自信，他们的安全预防措施也很随意。
en: He noticed at once that discipline was slack:the Danes had the self- confidence of conquerors,and their security precautions were casual.

zh: 他们靠袭击邻近地区的收入过着很好的生活。
en: They lived well,on the proceeds of raids on neighbouring regions.

zh: 他们在那里聚集了女人和食物和饮料，安逸的生活使他们变得柔软。
en: There they collected women as well as food and drink,and a life of ease had 10 made them soft.

zh: 阿尔弗雷德在返回阿瑟尔尼之前在营地待了一周。
en: Alfred stayed in the camp a week before he returned to Athelney.

zh: 与丹麦游牧部落相比，那里集结的兵力微不足道。
en: The force there assembled was trivial compared with the Danish horde.

zh: 但阿尔弗雷德推断丹麦人不再适合长期战斗：他们的粮食部门没有组织，只能依靠不定期的袭击。
en: But Alfred had deduced that the Danes were no longer fit for prolonged battle:and that their commissariat had no organization,but depended on irregular raids.

zh: 因此，面对丹麦人的进攻，阿尔弗雷德没有冒险公开战斗，而是骚扰敌人。
en: So,faced with the Danish advance,Alfred did not risk open battle but harried the enemy.

zh: 他不断地移动，吸引着丹麦人追随他。
en: He was constantly 15 on the move,drawing the Danes after him.

zh: 他的巡逻阻止了突袭队伍：饥饿袭击了丹麦军队。
en: His patrols halted the raiding parties:hunger assailed the Danish army.

zh: 现在，阿尔弗雷德开始了一系列的小冲突——不到一个月，丹麦人就投降了。
en: Now Alfred began a long series of skirmishes—and within a month the Danes had surrendered.

zh: 这一集可以说是一部独特的皇家间谍史诗！
en: The episode could reasonably serve as a unique epic of royal espionage!

zh: 伯纳德·纽曼 在英国的间谍 阿尔弗雷德，伪装成竖琴手，在丹麦人的营地 第 2 单元第 9 课
en: BERNARD NEWMAN Spies in Britain Alfred,disguised as a harpist,in the camp of the Danes Unit 2 Lesson 9

zh: 技术趋势可能会推动硅谷回到未来。
en: Technology trends may push Silicon Valley back to the future.

zh: 集成电路先驱、加州理工学院计算机科学教授卡弗·米德 (Carver Mead) 指出，现在的工作站使工程师能够在办公桌上设计、测试和生产芯片，就像编辑在麦金塔电脑上创建时事通讯一样。
en: Carver Mead,a pioneer in integrated circuits and a professor of computer science at the California Institute of Technology,notes there are now work- stations that enable engineers to design,test and produce chips right on their desks,much the way an editor creates a newsletter on a Macintosh.

zh: 随着制造芯片的时间和成本下降到几天和几百美元，工程师们很快就可以自由地发挥他们的想象力，而不会因昂贵的失败而受到惩罚。
en: As the time and cost of making a chip drop to a few days and a few hundred dollars,engineers may soon be free to let their imaginations soar without being penalized by expensive failures.

zh: 米德预测，发明家将能够在办公室一个周末完善强大的定制芯片，从而催生新一代车库初创企业，并为美国带来新的机遇。
en: Mead predicts that inventors will be able to perfect powerful customized chips over a weekend at the office—spawning a new generation of garage start-ups and giving the U.

zh: 这将使美国在将新产品快速推向市场方面领先于其外国竞争对手。
en: S.a jump on its foreign rivals in getting new products to market fast.

zh: “我们有更多的车库，里面都是聪明人，”米德说。
en: 'We've got more garages with smart people,'Mead observes.

zh: “我们确实在无政府状态下茁壮成长。
en: 'We really thrive on anarchy.

zh: ’还有亚洲人。
en: ' And on Asians.

zh: 东方人和亚裔美国人已经构成许多硅谷公司的工程人员的大多数。
en: Already,orientals and Asian Americans constitute the majority of the engineering staffs at many Valley firms.

zh: 中国、韩国、菲律宾和印度工程师正成群结队地从加州大学毕业。
en: And Chinese,Korean,Filipino and Indian engineers are graduating in droves from California's colleges.

zh: 作为下一代初创企业的负责人，这些亚洲创新者可以利用习俗和语言与重要的环太平洋市场建立更紧密的联系。
en: As the heads of next-generation start-ups,these Asian innovators can draw on customs and languages to forge tighter links with crucial Pacific Rim markets.

zh: 例如，斯坦福大学博士 Alex Au。来自香港，
en: For instance,Alex Au,a Stanford Ph.

zh: 已在台湾设立工厂，挑战日本近乎锁定的存储芯片市场。
en: D.from Hong Kong,has set up a Taiwan factory to challenge Japan's near lock on the memory- chip market.

zh: 印度出生的N.
en: India-born N.

zh: 去年春天，达莫达尔·雷迪 (Damodar Reddy) 的加州小公司在密苏里州的资助下，在堪萨斯城重新开设了一家 AT&T 芯片工厂。
en: Damodar Reddy's tiny California company reopened an AT&T chip plant in Kansas City last spring with financing from the state of Missouri.

zh: 在成为退休村之前，硅谷可能会成为建立全球企业的课堂。
en: Before it becomes a retirement village, Silicon Valley may prove a classroom for building a global business.

zh: 美国新闻与世界报道，1989 年 10 月 2 日
en: US NEWS AND WoRLD REPORT,October 2,1989

zh: 一些老人因对死亡的恐惧而感到压抑。
en: Some old people are oppressed by the fear of death.

zh: 对于年轻人来说，这种感觉是有道理的。
en: In the young there is a justification for this feeling.

zh: 有理由担心自己会战死沙场的年轻人可能会因为自己被欺骗而失去了生活所提供的最好的东西而感到痛苦。
en: Young men who have reason to fear that they will be killed in battle may justifiably feel bitter in the thought that they have been cheated of the best things that life has to offer.

zh: 但对于一个尝尽人间悲欢离合、完成了自己该做的事的老人来说，对死亡的恐惧就显得有些卑鄙和不光彩了。
en: But in an old man who has known human joys and sorrows,and has achieved whatever work it was in him to do,the fear of death is somewhat abject 5 and ignoble.

zh: 克服它的最好方法——至少在我看来——就是让你的兴趣逐渐更广泛、更客观，直到自我的围墙一点点消退，你的生活越来越融入普遍的生活。
en: The best way to overcome it—so at least it seems to me—is to make your interests gradually wider and more impersonal,until bit by bit the walls of the ego recede,and your life becomes increasingly merged in the universal life.

zh: 一个人的存在应该像一条河流——起初很小，被狭窄的河岸所包围，然后热情地流过巨石和瀑布。
en: An individual human existence should be like a river—small at first,narrowly contained within its banks,and rushing passionately past boulders and over waterfalls.

zh: 渐渐地，河面变宽，河岸后退，水流更加平静，最后，它们没有任何明显的断裂，融入大海，毫无痛苦地失去了个体的存在。
en: Gradually the river grows wider,the banks recede,the waters flow more quietly,and in the end,without any visible break,they 10 become merged in the sea,and painlessly lose their individual being.

zh: 一个人到了晚年，能够以这种方式看待自己的生活，就不会遭受对死亡的恐惧，因为他所关心的事情会继续下去。
en: The man who,in old age,can see his life in this way,will not suffer from the fear of death,since the things he cares for will continue.

zh: 如果随着活力的衰退，疲倦增加，休息的想法也不会不受欢迎。
en: And if,with the decay of vitality,weariness increases,the thought of rest will be not unwelcome.

zh: 我希望在工作中死去，知道其他人会继续做我不再能做的事情，并满足于认为可能的事情已经完成了。
en: I should wish to die while still at work,knowing that others will carry on what I can no longer do,and content in the thought that what was possible has been done.

zh: 伯特兰·罗素 如何从记忆中的肖像中变老 伯特兰·罗素在特拉法加广场的抗议集会上讲话 第 2 单元 第 11 课
en: BERTRAND RuSSELL How to grow old from Portraits from Memory Bertrand Russell speaking at a protest meeting in Trafalgar Square Unit 2 Lesson 11

zh: 银行与其客户彼此之间，负有多重权利义务关系。
en: 5 and its customer owe a large number of obligations to one another.

zh: 其中许多义务可能会引起问题和复杂化，但与商品买家不同，银行客户不能抱怨法律对他不利。
en: Many of these obligations can give rise to problems and complications but a bank customer,unlike,say,a buyer of goods,cannot complain that the law is loaded against him.

zh: 银行必须服从客户的指示，而不是其他任何人的指示。
en: The bank must obey its customer's instructions,and not those of anyone else.

zh: 例如，当客户第一次开立账户时，他指示银行仅将自己开出的支票从其账户中扣除。
en: When,for example,a customer first opens an account,he instructs the bank to debit his account only in respect of cheques drawn 10 by himself.

zh: 他向银行提供了他的签名样本，并且有一条非常严格的规则，即银行无权或授权在伪造客户签名的支票上支付客户的钱。
en: He gives the bank specimens of his signature,and there is a very firm rule that the bank has no right or authority to pay out a customer's money on a cheque on which its customer's signature has been forged.

zh: 伪造的技术可能非常高明，但这并没有什么区别：银行必须识别其客户的签名。
en: It makes no difference that the forgery may have been a very skilful one:the bank must recognize its customer's signature.

zh: 因此，银行采用的在支票上印上客户姓名的做法不会给客户带来任何风险。
en: For this reason there is no risk to the customer in the practice,adopted by banks,of printing the customer's name on his cheques.

zh: 如果这有利于伪造，损失的是银行，而不是客户。
en: If this facilitates forgery,it is the bank which will lose,not the customer.

zh: 戈登·巴里和奥布里·L.
en: GORDON BARRIE and AUBREYL.

zh: 钻石 消费者协会和法律 National Westminster Bank at Queen St.in Cardiff 第 2 单元第 12 课
en: DIAMOND The Consumer Society and the Law National Westminster Bank at Queen St.in Cardiff Unit 2 Lesson 12

zh: 最深的洞是为石油而挖的，深达 25,000 英尺。
en: The deepest holes of allare made for oil,and they go down to as much as 25,000 feet.

zh: 但我们不需要像开采其他矿藏那样派人下去开采石油。
en: But we do not need to send men down to get the oil out,as we must with other mineral deposits.

zh: 这些孔只是钻孔，直径不到一英尺。
en: The holes are only borings,less than a foot in diameter.

zh: 我的特殊经验主要是在石油方面，与任何其他采矿活动相比，寻找石油对改善深部钻探的贡献更大。
en: My particular experience is largely in oil,and the search for oil has done more to improve deep drilling than any other mining activity.

zh: 当决定了要在哪里钻探后，我们就在地面搭建了一个石油井架。
en: When it has been decided where we are going to drll, 5 we put up at the surface an oil derrick.

zh: 它必须很高，因为它就像一个巨大的滑轮组，我们必须降低到地面并从地面拖出很长的钻杆，这些钻杆由顶部的发动机旋转，并在底部装有切削钻头。
en: It has to be tallbecause it is like a giant block and tackle,and we have to lower into the ground and haul out of the ground great lengths of drill pipe which are rotated by an engine at the top and are fitted with a cutting bit at the bottom.

zh: 地质学家需要知道钻头已经到达了哪些岩石，因此时常使用取芯钻头获取样本。
en: The geologist needs to know what rocks the drillhas reached,so every so often a sample is obtained with a coring bit.

zh: 它切割出干净的圆柱体岩石，从中可以看到钻头已经切割过的地层。
en: It cuts a clean cylinder of rock,from which can be seen the strata the drill has been cutting 10 through.

zh: 一旦我们到达石油，它通常会流到地面，因为来自气体或水的巨大压力正在推动它。
en: Once we get down to the oil,it usually flows to the surface because great pressure,either from gas or water,is pushing it.

zh: 该压力必须受到控制，我们通过沿着钻杆循环的泥浆来控制它。
en: This pressure must be under control,and we control it by means of the mud which we circulate down the drill pipe.

zh: 我们努力避免传统的、浪漫的喷井观念，这种观念会浪费石油和天然气。
en: We endeavour to avoid the old,romantic idea of a gusher,which wastes oil and gas.

zh: 我们希望它留在洞里，直到我们能以可控的方式引导它离开。
en: We want it to stay down the hole until we can lead it off in a controlled manner.

zh: GASKELL 从 Discovery 中寻找地球矿物 波斯湾阿布扎比海上油田的石油钻井平台钻探 第 2 单元 第 13 课
en: GASKELL The Search for the Earth's Minerals from Discovery An oil rig drilling in the Abu Dhabi offshore oilfields in the Persian Gulf Unit 2 Lesson 13

zh: 超过两三天，世界上最好的天气预报都是推测性的，超过六七天就毫无价值了。
en: Beyond two or three days,the world's best weather forecasts are speculative,and beyond six or seven they are worthless.

zh: 蝴蝶效应就是原因。
en: The Butterfly Effect is the reason.

zh: 对于小天气——对于全球预报员来说，小天气可能意味着雷暴和暴风雪——任何预测都会迅速恶化。
en: For small pieces of weather—and to aglobal forecaster,smallcan mean thunderstorms and blizzards—any prediction deteriorates rapidly.

zh: 误差和不确定性成倍增加，通过一系列湍流特征向上级联，从尘暴和飑到只有卫星才能看到的大陆大小的漩涡。
en: Errors and uncertainties multiply, 5 cascading upward through a chain of turbulent features,from dust devils and squalls up to continent-size eddies that only satellites can see.

zh: 现代天气模型使用相距六十英里的点网格，即使如此，也必须猜测一些起始数据，因为地面站和卫星无法看到任何地方。
en: The modern weather models work with a grid of points of the order of sixty miles apart,and even so, some starting data has to be guessed,since ground stations and satellites cannot see everywhere.

zh: 但假设·地球上布满了​​间隔一英尺的传感器，并以一英尺的间隔一直上升到大气层的顶部。
en: But suppose · the earth could be covered with sensors spaced one foot apart,rising at one-foot intervals all the way to the 10 top of the atmosphere.

zh: 假设每个传感器都能提供完全准确的温度、压力、湿度以及气象学家想要的任何其他量的读数。
en: Suppose every sensor gives perfectly accurate readings of temperature,pressure, humidity,and any other quantity a meteorologist would want.

zh: 恰好在中午，一台功能无限的计算机会获取所有数据并计算 12.01、12.02、12.03 各时间点会发生什么……
en: Precisely at noon an infinitely powerful computer takes all the data and calculates what will happen at each point at 12.01,then 12.02,then 12.03 ...

zh: 计算机仍然无法预测一个月后新泽西州普林斯顿的某一天是否会晴天或下雨。
en: The computer will stillbe unable to predict whether Princeton,New Jersey,will have sun or rain on a day one month away.

zh: 中午，传感器之间的空间会隐藏计算机无法识别的波动，即与平均值的微小偏差。
en: At noon the spaces between the sensors will hide fluctuations that the computer will 15 not know about,tiny deviations from the average.

zh: 到 12.01，这些波动已经在一英尺之外造成了小误差。
en: By 12.01,those fluctuations will already have created small errors one foot away.

zh: 很快，误差就会扩大到十英尺的规模，依此类推，扩大到地球的大小。
en: Soon the errors will have multiplied to the ten-foot scale,and so on up to the size of the globe.

zh: 詹姆斯·格莱克《混沌》
en: JAMES GLEICK,Chaos

zh: 有两个因素严重影响工业科学研究的有效性。
en: Two factors weigh heavily against the effectiveness of scientific research in industry.

zh: 一是研究进行时普遍的保密气氛，二是研究人员个人缺乏自由。
en: One is the general atmosphere of secrecy in which it is carried out,the other the lack of freedom of the individual research worker.

zh: 只要任何调查都是秘密的，它自然会限制所有参与调查的人员与其他国家或大学的科学家同事进行有效的接触，甚至经常与同一公司的其他部门的科学家进行有效接触。
en: In so far as any inquiry is a secret one,it naturally limits all those engaged in carrying it out from effective contact with their fellow scientists either in other countries or in universities,or even,often enough, 5 in other departments of the same firm.

zh: 保密程度自然差异很大。
en: The degree of secrecy naturally varies considerably.

zh: 一些较大的公司从事的研究具有普遍性和基础性，因此不保守秘密对他们来说是一个积极的优势。
en: Some of the bigger firms are engaged in researches which are of such general and fundamental nature that it is a positive advantage to them not to keep them secret.

zh: 然而，许多依赖于此类研究的过程都是在完全保密的情况下进行的，直到可以申请专利的阶段为止。
en: Yet a great many processes depending on such research are sought for with complete secrecy until the stage at which patents can be taken out.

zh: 更多的工艺根本没有申请专利，而是作为秘密工艺保存。
en: Even more processes are never patented at all but kept as secret processes.

zh: 这尤其适用于化学工业，其中偶然发现比物理和机械工业发挥更大的作用。
en: This applies particularly to chemical industries,where 10 chance discoveries play a much larger part than they do in physical and mechanical industries.

zh: 有时，保密程度达到了无法提及研究的全部性质的程度。
en: Sometimes the secrecy goes to such an extent that the whole nature of the research cannot be mentioned.

zh: 例如，许多公司很难从图书馆获取技术或科学书籍，因为他们不愿意将自己的名字输入为已取出某本书，因为担心其他公司的代理人应该能够追踪他们可能正在进行的研究类型。
en: Many firms, for instance,have great difficulty in obtaining technical or scientific books from libraries because they are unwilling to have their names entered as having taken out such and such a book,for fear the agents of other firms should be able to trace the kind of research they are likely to be undertaking.

zh: BERNAL 科学的社会功能 一位在柴郡“阿克顿”实验室工作的科学家 第 2 单元第 15 课
en: BERNAL The Social Function of Science A scientist working at the ‘Arcton'Laboratories in Cheshire Unit 2 Lesson 15

zh: 在工业生活的组织中，工厂对工人生理和心理状态的影响被完全忽视了。
en: In the organization of industrial life the influence of the factory upon the physiological and mental state of the workers has been completely neglected.

zh: 现代工业基于以最低成本获得最大产量的理念，以便个人或一群人可以赚到尽可能多的钱。
en: Modern industry is based on the conception of the maximum production at lowest cost,in order that an individual or a group of individuals may earn as much money as possible.

zh: 它的扩张完全没有考虑到操作机器的人类的真实本质，也没有考虑到工厂强加的人工生存模式对个人及其后代产生的影响。
en: It has expanded without any idea of the true nature of the human beings who run the machines,and 5 without giving any consideration to the effects produced on the individuals and on their descendants by the artificial mode of existence imposed by the factory.

zh: 大城市的建造并没有考虑到我们。
en: The great cities have been built with no regard for us.

zh: 摩天大楼的形状和尺寸完全取决于获得每平方英尺土地最大收入的必要性，以及为租户提供令他们满意的办公室和公寓的必要性。
en: The shape and dimensions of the skyscrapers depend entirely on the necessity of obtaining the maximum income per square foot of ground,and of offering to the tenants offices and apartments that please them.

zh: 这导致了巨大的建筑物的建造，太多的人挤在一起。
en: This caused the construction of gigantic buildings where too large masses of human beings are crowded 10 together.

zh: 文明人喜欢这样的生活方式。
en: Civilized men like such a way of living.

zh: 当他们享受住所的舒适和平庸的奢华时，他们没有意识到他们被剥夺了生活必需品。
en: While they enjoy the comfort and banal luxury of their dwelling,they do not realize that they are deprived of the necessities of life.

zh: 现代城市由巨大的大厦和充满汽油味和有毒气体的黑暗狭窄的街道组成，被出租车、卡车和公共汽车的噪音撕裂，并且不断地挤满了人群。
en: The modern city consists of monstrous edifices and of dark,narrow streets full of petrol fumes and toxic gases,torn by the noise of the taxicabs,lorries and buses,and thronged ceaselessly by great crowds.

zh: 显然，这并不是为了居民的利益而规划的。
en: Obviously,it has not been planned for the good of its inhabitants.

zh: ALEXIS CARREL'Man，未知者 宾夕法尼亚州匹兹堡的鸟瞰图 第 2 单元 第 16 课
en: ALEXIS CARREL'Man,the Unknown An aerial veiw of Pittsburgh,Pennsylvania Unit 2 Lesson 16

zh: 在澳大利亚定居初期，有进取心的定居者不明智地引入了欧洲兔。
en: In the early days of the settlement of Australia,enterprising settlers unwisely introduced the European rabbit.

zh: 这种兔子在对足动物中没有天敌，因此它以兔子的杂乱抛弃特性繁殖。
en: This rabbit had no natural enemies in the Antipodes,so that it multiplied with that promiscuous abandon characteristic of rabbits.

zh: 它覆盖了整个大陆。
en: It overran a whole continent.

zh: 它通过挖洞和吞噬原本可以养活数百万只羊和牛的牧草而造成破坏。
en: It caused devastation by burrowing and by devouring the herbage which might have maintained millions of sheep and cattle.

zh: 科学家发现，这种特殊品种的兔子（显然没有其他动物）容易感染致命的病毒性疾病——粘液瘤病。
en: Scientists discovered that this particular variety of rabbit(and apparently no other animal)was susceptible to a fatal virus disease, myxomatosis.

zh: 通过感染动物并将它们释放到洞穴中，可能会造成这种疾病的局部流行。
en: By infecting animals and letting them loose in the burrows,local epidemics of this disease could be created.

zh: 后来发现有一种蚊子是这种疾病的携带者，并将其传染给兔子。
en: Later it was found that there was a type of mosquito which acted as the carrier of this disease and passed it on to the rabbits.

zh: 因此，当世界其他国家都在努力消灭蚊子时，澳大利亚却在鼓励这种蚊子。
en: So while the rest of the world was trying to get rid of mosquitoes, Australia was encouraging this one.

zh: 它有效地将疾病传播到整个大陆，并大幅减少了兔子的数量。
en: It effectively spread the disease all over the continent and drastically reduced the rabbit population.

zh: 后来发现兔子对这种疾病产生了一定程度的抵抗力，因此兔子种群不太可能被完全消灭。
en: It later became apparent that rabbits were developing a degree of resistance to this disease,so that the rabbit population was unlikely to be completely exterminated.

zh: 然而，人们希望兔子的问题能够得到解决。
en: There were hopes, however,that the problem of the rabbit would become manageable.

zh: 具有讽刺意味的是，欧洲将兔子作为害虫遗赠给澳大利亚，却将这种人造疾病作为瘟疫传染给了澳大利亚。
en: Ironically,Europe,which had bequeathed the rabbit as a pest to Australia,acquired this man-made disease as a pestilence.

zh: 一位法国医生决定消灭他自己庄园里的野兔，并引入了粘液瘤病。
en: A French physician decided to get rid of the wild rabbits on his own estate and introduced myxomatosis.

zh: 然而，它并不属于他的庄园范围。
en: It did not,however,remain within the confines of his estate.

zh: 它传播到了法国，在法国，野兔通常不被视为害虫，而是一种运动和有用的食物供应；它还传播到了英国，在英国，野兔被视为害虫，但同样容易感染这种疾病的家兔是有利可图的毛皮工业的基础。
en: It spread through France,where wild rabbits are not generally regarded as a pest but as a sport and a useful food supply,and it spread to Britain where wild rabbits are regarded as a pest but where domesticated rabbits,equally suscep- tible to the disease,are the basis of a profitable fur industry.

zh: 问题之一是人类能否控制他发明的疾病。
en: The question became one of whether Man could control the disease he had invented.

zh: 里奇·卡尔德 科学有道理
en: RITCHIE CALDER Science Makes Sense

zh: 我们也不必假设他们这样做主要是出于好奇或为了消遣，比如乘船游览船头的波浪。
en: 5 being they have much more likely done it out of curiosity or for sport,as in riding the bow waves of a ship.

zh: 1928 年，一些海豚被拍到像海狸一样将浸水的床垫推上岸。
en: In 1928 some porpoises were photographed working like beavers to push ashore a waterlogged mattress.

zh: 如果真如报道的那样，它们保护了人类免受鲨鱼的侵害，那可能是因为好奇心吸引了它们，也可能是因为可能的食物的气味吸引了鲨鱼。
en: If, as has been reported,they have protected humans from sharks,it may have been because curiosity attracted them and because the scent of a possible meal attracted the sharks.

zh: 海豚和鲨鱼是天敌。
en: Porpoises and sharks are natural enemies.

zh: 在这种情况下，可能会发生一场战斗，鲨鱼被赶走或杀死。
en: It is possible that upon such an occasion a battle ensued,with the sharks being driven away or killed.

zh: 无论是鸟、鱼还是兽，海豚对任何有生命的东西都感兴趣。
en: Whether it be bird,fish or beast,the porpoise is intrigued with anything that is alive.

zh: 他们不断地追捕海龟，而海龟却平静地接受了各种侮辱。
en: They are constantly after the turtles,who peacefully submit to all sorts of indignities.

zh: 一只小牛特别喜欢用鼻子把海龟举到水面，然后像滑水机一样将它推过水箱。
en: One young calf especially enjoyed raising a turtle to the surface with his snout and then shoving him across the tank like an aquaplane.

zh: 几乎每天，人们都会看到一只年幼的江豚试图将一只 300 磅重的海龟翻过来，将鼻子伸到贝壳的边缘下，拼命挣扎。
en: Almost any day a young porpoise may be seen trying to turn a 300-pound sea turtle over by sticking his snout under the edge of his shelland pushing up for dear life.

zh: 这并不容易，可能需要两只海豚一起工作。
en: This is not easy,and may require two porpoises working together.

zh: 在另一场游戏中，当海龟游过海洋馆时，第一只鼠海豚从上方俯冲下来，用腹部抵住龟壳。
en: In another game,as the turtle swims across the oceanarium,the first porpoise swoops down from above and butts his shell with his belly.

zh: 这将乌龟击落了几英尺。
en: This knocks the turtle down several feet.

zh: 他刚恢复平衡，下一只海豚就出现并再次击中他。
en: He no sooner recovers his equilibrium than the next porpoise comes along and hits him another crack.

zh: 最终，乌龟被一路撞到了水箱的地板上。
en: Eventually the turtle has been butted all the way down to the floor of the tank.

zh: 现在他只要试着站起来就心满意足了，但当他站起来时，一只海豚把他撞倒了。
en: He is now satisfied merely to try to stand up,but as soon as he does so a porpoise knocks him flat.

zh: 乌龟最终放弃了，把脚拉到龟壳下面，游戏结束了。
en: The turtle at last gives up by pulling his feet under his shelland the game is over.

zh: 拉尔夫·纳丁山 海中之窗
en: RALPH NADING HILL Window in the Sea

zh: 很明显，睡眠时间一定有某种功能，而且由于睡眠时间如此之多，所以这种功能似乎很重要。
en: It is fairly clear that the sleeping period must have some function,and because there is so much of it the function would seem to be important.

zh: 关于睡眠本质的猜测已经持续了数千年，而一个让这个问题令人费解的奇怪发现是，睡眠看起来似乎不仅仅是让身体休息的问题。
en: Speculations about its nature have been going on for literally thousands of years,and one odd finding that makes the problem puzzling is that it looks very much as if sleeping is not simply a matter of giving the body a rest.

zh: “休息”，即肌肉放松等，可以通过短暂的躺着，甚至坐着来实现。
en: 'Rest',in terms of muscle relaxation and so on,can be achieved by a brief period lying,or even sitting down.

zh: 身体的组织在一定程度上具有自我修复和自我恢复的能力，当或多或少持续活跃时，其功能最佳。
en: The body's tissues are self-repairing and self-restoring to a degree,and function best when more or less continuously active.

zh: 事实上，睡眠期间会发生基本的运动量，这特别与防止肌肉不活动有关。
en: In fact a basic amount of movement occurs during sleep which is specifically concerned with preventing muscle inactivity.

zh: 如果不是身体休息的问题，那么或许是大脑需要休息？
en: If it is not a question of resting the body,then perhaps it is the brain that needs resting?

zh: 如果没有两个因素的话，这可能是一个合理的假设。
en: This might be a plausible hypothesis were it not for two factors.

zh: 首先，脑电图仪（只是一种通过将电极连接到头皮上来记录大脑电活动的设备）显示，虽然睡眠期间的活动模式发生了变化，但没有证据表明活动总量有所减少。
en: First the electroencephalograph(which is simply a device for recording the electrical activity of the brain by attaching electrodes to the scalp)shows that while there is a change in the pattern of activity during sleep,there is no evidence that the total amount of activity is any less.

zh: 第二个因素更有趣，也更基本。
en: The second factor is more interesting and more fundamental.

zh: 几年前，一位名叫威廉·德门特的美国精神病学家发表了记录睡眠期间眼球运动的实验。
en: Some years ago an American psychiatrist named William Dement published experiments dealing with the recording of eye-movements during sleep.

zh: 他表明，普通人的睡眠周期会不时出现特殊的眼球运动，有些是漂移且缓慢的，另一些是不稳定且快速的。
en: He showed that the average individual's sleep cycle is punctuated with peculiar bursts of eye-movements, some drifting and slow,others jerky and rapid.

zh: 在这些眼球运动期间醒来的人通常报告说他们一直在做梦。
en: People woken during these periods of eye-movements generally reported that they had been dreaming.

zh: 当其他时间醒来时，他们报告说没有做梦。
en: When woken at other times they reported no dreams.

zh: 如果一组人连续几个晚上的眼动睡眠受到干扰，而另一组人在同样的时间内受到干扰，但当他们没有表现出眼动睡眠时，第一组人开始表现出一些人格障碍，而其他人似乎或多或少不受影响。
en: If one group of people were disturbed from their eye-movement sleep for several nights on end,and another group were disturbed for an equal period of time but when they were not exhibiting eye-movements,the first group began to show some personality disorders while the others seemed more or less unaffected.

zh: 所有这一切的含义是，重要的不是睡眠的干扰，而是梦的干扰。
en: The implications of all this were that it was not the disturbance of sleep that mattered,but the disturbance of dreaming.

zh: 克里斯托弗·埃文斯《倾听者》昼夜节律实验中的梦想之物。
en: CHRISTOPHER EvANS The stuff of dreams from The Listener Circadian rhythm experiment.

zh: 一名患者在睡眠研究实验室里熟睡。
en: A patient is asleep in a sleep research laboratory.

zh: 电极连接到他的头上来测量脑电波。
en: Electrodes are attached to his head to measure brainwaves.

zh: 第 3 单元第 19 课
en: Unit 3 Lesson 19

zh: 凭借它们所拥有的下颚，蛇可以毫不费力地吃掉食物——不超过一口。
en: 5 food with very little effort,no more effort than one bite.

zh: 为什么只有蛇？
en: And why only snakes?

zh: 例如，猫会得到很大的帮助；不需要与大型、凶猛的老鼠打架，也不需要与成年兔子打架——只需咬一口，不需要更多的努力。
en: Cats,for instance,would be greatly helped;no running fights with large,fierce rats or tussles with grown rabbits—just a bite and no more effort needed.

zh: 事实上，它对所有食肉动物来说都是一种帮助，尽管在它们互相争斗时它是一把两刃武器。
en: In fact,it would be an assistance to all carnivores though it would be a two-edged weapon when they fought each other.

zh: 但是，在脊椎动物中，不可预测的大自然只选择了蛇（和一只蜥蜴）。
en: But,of the vertebrates,unpredictable Nature selected only snakes(and one lizard).

zh: 人们还想知道为什么大自然和一些蛇一起调制出如此威力的毒药。
en: One wonders also why Nature,with some snakes,concocted poison of such extreme potency.

zh: 在唾液转化为毒物的过程中，人们可能会认为发生了一个固定的过程。
en: In the conversion of saliva into poison,one might suppose that a fixed process took place.

zh: 事实并非如此；有些蛇制造的毒药在各方面都与其他蛇不同，就像砷与士的宁不同一样，并且具有不同的效果。
en: It did not;some snakes manufactured a poison different in every respect from that of others,as different as arsenic is from strychnine,and having different effects.

zh: 一种毒物作用于神经，另一种毒物作用于血液。
en: One poison acts on the nerves,the other on the blood.

zh: 神经毒的制造者包括曼巴蛇和眼镜蛇，它们的毒液被称为神经毒。
en: The makers of the nerve poison include the mambas and the cobras and their venom is called neurotoxic.

zh: 蝰蛇和响尾蛇会产生血液毒物，称为溶血性毒物。
en: Vipers(adders)and rattlesnakes manufacture the blood poison,which is known as haemolytic.

zh: 这两种毒都令人不快，但迄今为止更令人不快的是血毒。
en: Both poisons are unpleasant,but by far the more unpleasant is the blood poison.

zh: 据说神经毒是两者中更原始的，血液毒可以说是改进配方的新产品。
en: It is said that the nerve poison is the more primitive of the two,that the blood poison is,so to speak,a newer product from an improved formula.

zh: 尽管如此，神经毒物对人的作用比血液毒物要快得多。
en: Be that as it may,the nerve poison does its business with man far more quickly than the blood poison.

zh: 然而，这没有任何意义。
en: This, however,means nothing.

zh: 蛇获得毒液并不是为了对付人类，而是为了对付老鼠等猎物，而蝰蛇毒液对这些猎物的作用几乎是立竿见影的。
en: Snakes did not acquire their poison for use against man but for use against prey such as rats and mice,and the effects on these of viperine poison is almost immediate.

zh: 约翰·克朗普顿 蛇
en: JOHN CROMPTON The snake

zh: 威廉·S。
en: William S.

zh: 哈特也许是所有西部片明星中最伟大的，因为与加里·库珀和约翰·韦恩不同，他只出现在西部片中。
en: Hart was,perhaps,the greatest of all Western stars,for unlike Gary Cooper and John Wayne he appeared in nothing but Westerns.

zh: 从1914年到1924年，他处于至高无上的地位，无人能挑战。
en: From 1914 to 1924 he was supreme and unchallenged.

zh: 正是哈特创造了西部片的基本模式，并设计了他在每一部电影中扮演的主角，好人与坏人，意外的高贵亡命之徒，或者诚实但被陷害的牛仔，或者被恶意流言蜚语所怀疑的警长；简而言之，就是与自己和边疆环境发生冲突的个体。
en: It was Hart who created the basic formula of the Western film,and devised the protagonist he played in every film he made, the good-bad man,the accidental-noble outlaw,or the honest-but-framed cowboy,or the sheriff made suspect by vicious gossip;in short,the individual in conflict with himself and his frontier environment.

zh: 与好莱坞的大多数同时代人不同，哈特实际上对旧西部有所了解。
en: Unlike most of his contemporaries in Hollywood,Hart actually knew something of the old West.

zh: 当他还是个孩子的时候，当它已经消失时，他就生活在其中，他的英雄深深地扎根于他的记忆和经历中，扎根于消失的边疆的历史和神话中。
en: He had lived in it as a child when it was already disappearing,and his hero was firmly rooted in his memories and experiences,and in both the history and the mythology of the vanished frontier.

zh: 尽管美国历史上没有哪个时期或地方比这更荒谬地浪漫化，但神话和现实至少在一个舞台上携手并进，即个人与入侵文明之间的冲突。
en: And although no period or place in American history has been more absurdly romanticized,myth and reality did join hands in at least one arena,the conflict between the individual and encroaching civilization.

zh: 习惯于与自然环境作斗争的人和印第安人都被政客、银行家和商人迷惑了，并被栅栏、法律和外来禁忌所束缚。
en: Men accustomed to struggling for survival against the elements and Indians were bewildered by politicians,bankers and businessmen,and unhorsed by fences,laws and alien taboos.

zh: 哈特笔下的好坏人始终是一个局外人，总是一个被剥夺继承权的人，如果他发现有必要一路上射杀治安官或抢劫银行，他的早期观众发现很容易理解和原谅，特别是当哈特最终战胜了进攻的印第安人时。
en: Hart's good-bad man was always an outsider,always one of the disinherited,and if he found it necessary to shoot a sheriff or rob a bank along the way,his early audiences found it easy to understand and forgive,especially when it was Hart who,in the end,overcame the attacking Indians.

zh: 二十世纪第二个十年的观众发现，逃离到生活虽然艰苦但相对简单的时代是件愉快的事。
en: Audiences in the second decade of the twentieth century found it pleasant to escape to a time when life, though hard,was relatively simple.

zh: 我们仍然如此；生活在一个不公开的侵略、战争、虚伪、欺骗、无政府状态和即将发生的自焚成为我们日常生活一部分的世界，我们都希望有一个生活准则。
en: We still do;living in a world in which undeclared aggression,war, hypocrisy,chicanery,anarchy and impending immolation are part of our daily lives,we all want a code to live by.

zh: 卡尔·福尔曼的美德和《观察家报》的快枪
en: CARL FoREMAN Virtue and a Fast Gun from The Observer

zh: 它们只能通过言语的方式与他人交流。
en: 5 be communicated to another by means of speech.

zh: 随着文字的发明，取得了巨大的进步，因为知识不仅可以交流，还可以存储。
en: With the invention of writing,a great advance was made, for knowledge could then be not only communicated but also stored.

zh: 图书馆使教育成为可能，而教育反过来又为图书馆增光添彩：知识的增长遵循一种复利规律，而印刷术的发明大大增强了这种规律。
en: Libraries made education possible,and education in its turn aded to libraries:the growth of knowledge followed a kind of compound interest law, which was greatly enhanced by the invention of printing.

zh: 这一切都比较缓慢，直到随着科学的到来，节奏突然加快了。
en: All this was comparatively slow until,with the coming of science,the tempo was suddenly raised.

zh: 然后知识开始按照系统的计划积累。
en: Then knowledge began to be accumulated according to a 10 systematic plan.

zh: 细流变成了溪流；溪流现在变成了激流。
en: The trickle became a stream;the stream has now become a torrent.

zh: 而且，一旦获得新知识，它就会立即转化为实际用途。
en: Moreover,as soon as new knowledge is acquired,it is now turned to practical account.

zh: 所谓“现代文明”并不是人性均衡发展的结果，而是积累的知识应用于实际生活的结果。
en: What is called ‘modern civilization'is not the result of a balanced development ofall man's nature,but of accumulated knowledge applied to practical life.

zh: 人类现在面临的问题是：如何利用所有这些知识？
en: The problem now facing humanity is:What is going to be done with all this knowledge?

zh: 正如人们经常指出的那样，知识是一把双刃武器，既可以用于善行，也可以用于作恶。
en: As is so often pointed out,knowledge is a two-edged weapon which can be used equallyfor good or evil.

zh: 现在它对两者都被漠不关心地使用。
en: It is now being 15 used indifferently for both.

zh: 例如，还有什么奇观比炮手利用科学粉碎人类的身体，而外科医生近在咫尺地利用科学来恢复身体的景象更残酷、异想天开吗？
en: Could any spectacle,for instance,be more grimly whimsical than that of gunners using science to shatter men's bodies while,close at hand,surgeons use it to restore them?

zh: 我们必须非常严肃地问自己，如果知识的这种双重使用及其不断增长的力量继续下去，将会发生什么。
en: We have to ask ourselves very seriously what will happen if this twofold use of knowledge,with its ever-increasing power, continues.

zh: 泰瑞尔 人的性格 军事野战医院 第三单元 第 22 课
en: TYRRELL The Personality of Man A military field-hospital Unit 3 Lesson 22

zh: 没有两种鸟具有完全相同的飞行方式；种类是无限的；但可以粗略地看出两个类别。
en: No two sorts of birds practise quite the same sort of flight;the varieties are infinite;but two classes may be roughly seen.

zh: 任何穿越太平洋的船只都会有较小的信天翁陪伴许多天，它可能会与船只陪伴一个小时，而不会看到或只是偶尔移动翅膀。
en: Any ship that crosses the Pacific is accompanied for many days by the smaller albatross, which may keep company with the vessel for an hour without visible or more than occasional movement of wing.

zh: 船壁向上的气流以及其航线上的气流足以为这只拥有巨大翅膀的巨鸟提供足够的食物和进步。
en: The currents of air that the walls of the ship direct upwards,as well as in the line of its course,are 5 enough to give the great bird with its immense wings sufficient sustenance and progress.

zh: 信天翁是滑翔机之王，这一类飞行者利用空气达到其目的，但必须屈服于空气的反对。
en: The albatross is the king of the gliders,the class of fliers which harness the air to their purpose,but must yield to its opposition.

zh: 在相反的学校里，鸭子是至高无上的。
en: In the contrary school,the duck is supreme.

zh: 正如他所吹嘘的那样，它更接近人类“征服”空气的引擎。
en: It comes nearer to the engines with which man has 'conquered' the air,as he boasts.

zh: 鸭子和鸽子一样，都拥有钢铁般的肌肉，这些肌肉占了鸟的重量的很大一部分，这些肌肉使短翅膀具有不可抗拒的力量，使得它们可以在疲惫之前在逆风中飞很长的距离。
en: Duck,and like them the pigeons,are endowed with steel-like muscles,that are a good part of the weight of the bird,and these willply the short wings with such irresistible power that they can 10 bore for long distances through an opposing gale before exhaustion follows.

zh: 它们的卑微追随者，如鹧鸪，也有类似的强大推进力，但很快就会疲倦。
en: Their humbler followers,such as partridges,have a like power of strong propulsion,but soon tire.

zh: 如果海上的风把它们赶上了长途旅行，你可能会在极度疲惫的情况下接起它们。
en: You may pick them up in utter exhaus- tion,if wind over the sea has driven them to a long journey.

zh: 燕子在很大程度上分享了两所学校的优点。
en: The swallow shares the virtues of both schools in highest measure.

zh: 它不疲倦，也不吹嘘自己的力量；而是属于空气，它往返于北方筑巢地的路程可能有六千英里，在飞行时喂养它的幼崽，并滑过一种即使在逆风时也能帮助它通过的介质。
en: It tires not,nor does it boast of its power;but belongs to the air,travelling it may be six thousand miles to and from its northern nesting home,feeding its flown young as it flies,and slipping 15 through a medium that seems to help its passage even when the wind is adverse.

zh: 这些鸟对我们有好处，尽管我们不再从它们的飞来飞去中获取预兆；甚至最迷信的村民也不再向喜鹊脱帽祝它早安。
en: Such birds do us good, though we no longer take omens from their flight on this side and that;and even the most superstitious villagers no longer take off their hats to the magpie and wish it good-morning.

zh: 威廉·比奇·托马斯 乡下人的信条 1 一群飞翔的大雁 第 3 单元 第 23 课
en: WILLIAM BEACH THOMAS A Countryman's Creed 1 A flock of wild geese in flight Unit 3 Lesson 23

zh: 一个年轻人看到日落，无法理解或表达它在他心中激起的情感，得出的结论是，它一定是通往彼岸世界的大门。
en: A young man sees a sunset and,unable to understand or to express the emotion that it rouses in him, concludes that it must be the gateway to a world that lies beyond.

zh: 我们中的任何人在强烈的审美体验时刻都很难抗拒这样的暗示：我们正在瞥见从不同的存在领域照射到我们身上的光，不同的，并且因为体验是强烈感人的，在某种程度上更高。
en: It is difficult for any of us in moments of intense aesthetic experience to resist the suggestion that we are catching a glimpse of a light that shines down to us from a different realm of existence,different and,because the experience is intensely moving,in some way higher.

zh: 而且，尽管这些光芒令人眼花缭乱，但它们所传达的美丽和宁静比我们所知道或想象的要大。
en: And,though the gleams blind and dazzle,yet do they convey a hint of beauty and serenity greater than we have known or imagined.

zh: 其意义也超出了我们所能描述的范围；因为语言是为了传达这个世界的意义而发明的，但它不能轻易地适应另一个世界的用途。
en: Greater too than we can describe;for language,which was invented to convey the meanings of this world,cannot readily be fitted to the uses of another.

zh: 不可否认的是，所有伟大的艺术都具有暗示彼岸世界的力量。
en: That all great art has this power of suggesting a world beyond is undeniable.

zh: 在某些情绪下，大自然也有同样的感受。
en: In some moods,Nature shares it.

zh: 六月的天空没有那么蓝，不会指向更蓝的地方；没有美丽的夕阳，不会唤醒更美的憧憬；那份美好的憧憬，还未完全瞥见，便已逝去，匆匆而过，留下了说不清的向往和遗憾。
en: There is no sky in June so blue that it does not point forward to a bluer,no sunset so beautiful that it does not waken the vision of a greater beauty,a vision which passes before it is fully glimpsed,and in passing leaves an indefinable longing and regret.

zh: 但是，如果这个世界不只是一个糟糕的笑话，生活只是星星冷光中的庸俗闪光，存在只是穿越神秘的空洞笑声；如果这些背后和超越的暗示不是消化不良产生的邪恶幽默，或者魔鬼发出的奇思妙想来嘲笑和疯狂我们，如果总而言之，美意味着某种东西，但我们决不能试图解释其意义。
en: But,if this world is not merely a bad joke,life a vulgar flare amid the cool radiance of the stars,and existence an empty laugh braying across the mysteries;if these intimations of a something behind and beyond are not evil humour born of indigestion,or whimsies sent by the devil to mock and madden us,if,in a word,beauty means something,yet we must not seek to interpret the meaning.

zh: 如果我们瞥见了无法言喻的事物，那么试图说出它是不明智的，我们也不应该试图赋予我们无法理解的事物以意义。
en: If we glimpse the unutterable,it is unwise to try to utter it,nor should we seek to invest with significance that which we cannot grasp.

zh: 美对于我们人类的意义来说是毫无意义的。
en: Beauty in terms of our human meanings is meaningless.

zh: JoAD 心事
en: JoAD Pieces of Mind

zh: 许多对噪声有实际经验的工业界和服务业人士认为，对这个问题的任何调查都是浪费时间；他们甚至不准备承认噪声影响人类的可能性。
en: Many people in industry and the Services,who have practical experience of noise,regard any investigation of this question as a waste of time;they are not prepared even to admit the possibility that noise affects people.

zh: 另一方面，那些不喜欢噪音的人有时会用最不充分的证据来支持他们对更安静的社会的呼吁。
en: On the other hand,those who dislike noise will sometimes use most inadequate evidence to support their pleas for a quieter society.

zh: 这很遗憾，因为减少噪音确实是一个很好的事业，如果它与糟糕的科学联系在一起，它可能会失去信誉。
en: This is a pity,because noise abatement really is a good cause,and it is likely 5 to be discredited if it gets to be associated with bad science.

zh: 一项经常被提出的指控是噪音会导致精神疾病。
en: One allegation often made is that noise produces mental illness.

zh: 例如，一份周报最近发表的一篇文章，在标题中添加了一幅引人注目的插图，描绘了一位处于相当痛苦状态的女士，标题是“她是又一个受害者，沦为尖叫的残骸”。
en: A recent article in a weekly newspaper, for instance,was headed with a striking illustration of a lady in a state of considerable distress,with the caption 'She was yet another victim,reduced to a screaming wreck’.

zh: 当急切地翻阅文字时，人们得知这位女士是一名打字员，她发现办公室打字机的声音让她越来越担心，直到最终她不得不住进精神病院。
en: On turning eagerly to the text,one learns that the lady was a typist who found the sound of office typewriters worried her more and more until 10 eventually she had to go into a mental hospital.

zh: 当然，这种轶事的问题在于人们无法区分因果关系。
en: Now the snag in this sort of anecdote is of course that one cannot distinguish cause and effect.

zh: 噪音是导致疾病的原因，还是对噪音的抱怨只是一种症状？
en: Was the noise a cause of the illness,or were the complaints about noise merely a symptom?

zh: 另一位病人也可能抱怨她的邻居联合起来诽谤她、迫害她，但人们可能对相信这种说法持谨慎态度。海军是研究噪声的好地方。
en: Another patient might equally well complain that her neighbours were combining to slander her and persecute her,and yet one might be cautious about believing this statement. navy is a good place to study noise.

zh: 但无论是精神病学访谈还是客观测试都无法显示出这些美国水手受到任何影响。
en: But neither psychiatric interviews nor objective tests were able to show 20 any effects upon these American sailors.

zh: 这一结果仅仅证实了美国和英国早期的研究：如果噪音对心理健康有任何影响，那么它一定很小，以至于现有的精神病诊断方法无法发现它。
en: This result merely confirms earlier American and British studies:if there is any effect of noise upon mental health,it must be so small that present methods of psychiatric diagnosis cannot find it.

zh: 这并不能证明它不存在；但它确实意味着噪音比在孤儿院长大的危险要小——孤儿院确实是一种心理健康危害。
en: That does not prove that it does not exist;but it does mean that noise is less dangerous than,say,being brought up in an orphanage—which really is a mental health hazard.

zh: 布罗德本特《科学调查》中噪音的非听觉影响
en: BROADBENT Non-auditory effects ofnoise from Science Survey

zh: 生活在水里或水附近的动植物遗骸最有可能被保存下来，因为保存的必要条件之一就是快速埋葬，只有在海洋、河流，有时还有湖泊等泥沙不断沉积的地方，尸体等才能被迅速覆盖和保存。
en: It is animals and plants which lived in or near water whose remains are most likely to be preserved,for one of the necessary conditions of preservation is quick burial,and it is only in the seas and rivers,and sometimes lakes,where mud and silt have been continuously deposited,that bodies and the like can be rapidly covered over and preserved.

zh: 但即使在最有利的情况下，只有一小部分死亡的生物在腐烂之前，或者更有可能的是，在食腐动物吃掉它们之前，以这种方式保存下来。
en: But even in the most favourable circumstances only a small fraction of the creatures that die are preserved in this way before decay sets in or,even more likely,before scavengers eat them.

zh: 毕竟，所有生物都靠吃其他东西而生存，无论是植物还是动物，死的还是活的，避免这样的命运只是偶然的。
en: After all,all living creatures live by feeding on something else,whether it be plant or animal,dead or alive,and it is only by chance that such a fate is avoided.

zh: 生活在陆地上的植物和动物的遗骸很少被保存下来，因为很少有任何东西可以覆盖它们。
en: The remains of plants and animals that lived on land are much moree rarely preserved,for there is seldom anything to cover them over.

zh: 当你想到无数飞来飞去的鸟类，更不用说同样数量众多的田鼠和田鼠等你看不到的小动物时，人们很少会遇到尸体，当然，除了在路上。
en: When you think of the innumerable birds that one sees flying about,not to mention the equally numerous small animals like field mice and voles which you do not see,it is very rarely that one comes across a dead body,except,of course,on the roads.

zh: 它们会分解并很快被天气破坏或被其他生物吃掉。
en: They decompose and are quickly destroyed by the weather or eaten by some other creature.

zh: 陆地动物的痕迹几乎总是由于一些非常特殊的情况而得以幸存，例如掉入难以进入的洞穴，或落入冰缝，如西伯利亚猛犸象，而有时整个动物被保存下来，如放在冰箱中。
en: It is almost always due to some very special circumstances that traces of land animals survive,as by falling into inaccessible caves,or into an ice crevasse,like the Siberian mammoths,when the whole animal is sometimes preserved,as in a refrigerator.

zh: 这就是著名的别列索夫卡猛犸象的遭遇，它被发现保存完好且状况良好。
en: This is what happened to the famous Beresovka mammoth which was found preserved and in good condition.

zh: 他嘴里含着冷杉树的残骸——这是他掉进裂缝、折断背部之前吃的最后一顿饭。
en: In his mouth were the remains of fir trees—the last meal that he had before he fell into the crevasse and broke his back.

zh: 这头猛犸象现已在圣路易斯古生物学博物馆中修复。
en: The mammoth has now been restored in the Palaeontological Museum in St.

zh: 圣彼得堡。
en: Petersburg.

zh: 其他动物也被困在焦油坑里，比如大象、剑齿虎，以及在兰乔拉布雷亚（现在只是洛杉矶郊区）发现的许多其他生物。
en: Other animals were trapped in tar pits,like the elephants,sabre- toothed cats,and numerous other creatures that are found at Rancho la Brea,which is now just a suburb of Los Angeles.

zh: 显然发生的事情是，水聚集在这些焦油坑上，像大象这样的大型动物冒险到明显坚硬的表面喝水，但很快就陷入了焦油之中。
en: Apparently what happened was that water collected on these tar pits,and the bigger animals like the elephants ventured out on to the apparently firm surface to drink,and were promptly bogged in the tar.

zh: 然后，当它们死后，剑齿虎、巨狼等食肉动物也出来觅食，也遭受了同样的命运。
en: And then,when they were dead,the carnivores,like the sabre-toothed cats and the giant wolves,came out to feed and suffered exactly the same fate.

zh: 焦油里也有无数的鸟类。
en: There are also endless numbers of birds in the tar as well.

zh: 埃罗尔·怀特（ERROL WHITE）：《发现》中地球的前世生活
en: ERROL WHITE The past life of the earth from Discovery

zh: 来自 17 世纪瑞典帝国的一艘大帆船在 1628 年处女航开始时沉没的故事一定是海上最奇怪的故事之一。
en: From the seventeenth-century empire of Sweden,the story of a galleon that sank at the start of her maiden voyage in 1628 must be one of the strangest tales of the sea.

zh: 她在斯德哥尔摩港的海底沉睡了近三个半世纪，直到 1956 年被发现。
en: For nearly three and a half centuries she lay at the bottom of Stockholm harbour until her discovery in 1956.

zh: 这是瓦萨号，伟大帝国舰队的皇家旗舰。
en: This was the Vasa,royal flagship of the great imperial fleet.

zh: 古斯塔夫·阿道夫国王，即“北方飓风”，当时正值三十年战争中军事胜利的鼎盛时期，他决定了她的尺寸和武器装备。
en: King Gustavus Adolphus,'The Northern Hurricane’,then at the height of his military success in the Thirty Years'War,had dictated her measurements and armament.

zh: 三重炮甲板上装有六十四门青铜大炮。
en: Triple gun-decks mounted sixty-four bronze cannon.

zh: 她的目的是在瑞典日益强大的力量中发挥领导作用。
en: She was intended to play a leading role in the growing might of Sweden.

zh: 1628 年 8 月 10 日，当她​​准备首次航行时，斯德哥尔摩正处于骚动之中。
en: As she was prepared for her maiden voyage on August 10,1628,Stockholm was in a ferment.

zh: 从斯科普斯布隆和周围的岛屿上，人们目睹了这个美丽的事物开始扬帆迎风。
en: From the Skeppsbron and surrounding islands the people watched this thing of beauty begin to spread her sails and 10 catch the wind.

zh: 他们花了三年的时间才制作出这艘漂浮的艺术品；她的雕刻和装饰比以前的任何一艘船都更加丰富。
en: They had laboured for three years to produce this floating work of art;she was more richly carved and ornamented than any previous ship.

zh: 高高的船尾城堡上雕刻着各种神灵、恶魔、骑士、国王、战士、美人鱼、小天使；动物形状的动物形状闪烁着红色、金色和蓝色的光芒，象征着勇气、力量和残酷，激发了当时迷信水手的想象力。
en: The high stern castle was a riot of carved gods,demons, knights,kings,warriors,mermaids,cherubs;and zoomorphic animal shapes ablaze with red and gold and blue,symbols of courage,power,and cruelty,were portrayed to stir the imaginations of the superstitious sailors of the day.

zh: 随后，停泊的战舰上的大炮发出雷鸣般的礼炮，瓦萨号也鸣枪回应。
en: Then the cannons of the anchored warships thundered a salute to which the Vasa fired in reply.

zh: 当她从飘散的炮烟云中出现时，船头下的水翻腾着泡沫，她的旗帜飘扬，三角旗飘扬，风帆在微风中飘扬，她的上层建筑的红色和金色闪闪发光，她呈现出斯德哥尔摩人以前从未见过的更加雄伟的景象。
en: As she emerged from her drifting cloud of gun smoke with the water churned to foam beneath her bow,her flags flying,pennants waving,sails filling in the breeze,and the red and gold of her superstructure ablaze with colour,she presented a more majestic spectacle than Stockholmers had ever seen before.

zh: 所有的炮口都敞开着，枪口邪恶地从炮口中探出头来。
en: All gun-ports were open and the muzzles peeped wickedly from them.

zh: 风刮得更猛烈了，突然刮起了狂风，船做出了奇怪的动作，向左舷倾斜。
en: As the wind freshened there came a sudden squall and the ship made a strange movement,listing to port.

zh: 军械官命令将所有左舷大炮移至右舷以抵消倾斜，但甲板的陡峭角度增加。
en: The Ordnance Officer ordered all the port cannon to be heaved to starboard to counteract the list,but the steepening angle of the decks increased.

zh: 然后隆隆的雷声传到了岸上的观察者那里，货物、压舱物、弹药和 400 人滑落到这艘陡峭倾斜的船的左舷。
en: Then the sound of rumbling thunder reached the watchers on the shore,as cargo,ballast,ammunition and 400 people went sliding and crashing down to the port side of the steeply listing ship.

zh: 下部的炮口现在已经在水下，涌入的水流决定了这艘船的命运。
en: The lower gun-ports were now below water and the inrush sealed the ship's fate.

zh: 在第一个辉煌的25小时里，原本打算统治波罗的海的强大的瓦萨号在她出生的港口沉没了，所有的旗帜都在飘扬。
en: In that 25 first glorious hour,the mighty Vasa,which was intended to rule the Baltic,sank with all flags flying—in the harbour of her birth.

zh: 罗伊·桑德斯 (Roy SAUNDERS) 《聆听者》中的《瓦萨号的崛起》
en: Roy SAUNDERS The Raising of the 'Vasa'from The Listener

zh: 这是一个怀疑论的时代，但是，尽管我们对许多我们的祖先所热切相信的事物的信心已经减弱，但我们对那瓶药的疗效的信心仍然和他们一样。
en: This is a sceptical age,but although our faith in many of the things in which our forefathers fervently believed has weakened,our confidence in the curative properties of the bottle of medicine remains the same as theirs.

zh: 卫生服务部门每年的药品费用已达到天文数字，而且目前还没有显示出停止增长的迹象，这一事实证明了现代人对药物的信心。
en: This modern faith in medicines is proved by the fact that the annual drug bill of the Health Services is mounting to astronomical figures and shows no signs at present of ceasing to rise.

zh: 大多数到我们医院门诊就诊的病人都觉得自己没有得到足够的治疗，除非他们能带一些有形的药物回家，比如一瓶药、一盒药片或一小罐药膏，而科室的负责医生也很乐意满足他们的这些要求。
en: The majority of the 5 patients attending the medical out-patients departments of our hospitals feel that they have not received adequate treatment unless they are able to carry home with them some tangible remedy in the shape of a bottle of medicine,a box of pills,or a small jar of ointment,and the doctor in charge of the department is only too ready to provide them with these requirements.

zh: 处理病人的方法没有比满足他们的要求更快的了，而且由于卫生服务部门的大多数医务人员都工作过度，几乎没有时间就饮食、正确的生活、放弃坏习惯等问题提供耗时且不被重视的建议，所以瓶子、盒子和罐子几乎总是给他们。
en: There is no quicker method of disposing of patients than by giving them what they are asking for,and since most medical men in the Health Services are 10 overworked and have little time for offering time-consuming and little-appreciated advice on such subjects as diet,right living,and the need for abandoning bad habits etc.,the bottle,the box,and the jar are almost always granted them.

zh: 并不是只有无知和没有受过教育的人才会对那瓶药抱有如此的信心。
en: Nor is it only the ignorant and ill-educated person who has such faith in the bottle of medicine.

zh: 据托马斯·卡莱尔回忆，当他听说他的朋友亨利·泰勒生病时，他立即去看望他，口袋里揣着一瓶以前为卡莱尔夫人的身体不适开的药的剩余部分。
en: It is recounted of Thomas Carlyle that when he heard of the illness of his friend,Henry Taylor,he went off 15 immediately to visit him,carrying with him in his pocket what remained of a bottle of medicine formerly prescribed for an indisposition of Mrs.

zh: （与上条末句连读为 Mrs. Carlyle's，即“卡莱尔夫人”。）
en: Carlyle's.

zh: 卡莱尔完全不知道他口袋里的瓶子里装的是什么，不知道他的朋友所患的疾病的性质，也不知道他的妻子以前出了什么问题，但是一种对一种疾病有效的药物肯定对另一种疾病也有同样的益处，想到他为他的朋友带来的帮助，他感到安慰，他急忙赶到亨利·泰勒的家。
en: Carlyle was entirely ignorant of what the bottle in his pocket contained,of the nature of the illness from which his friend was suffering,and of what had previously been wrong with his wife,but a medicine that had worked so well in one form of illness would surely be of equal benefit in another,and comforted by the thought of the help he was bringing to his friend, 20 he hastened to Henry Taylor's house.

zh: 历史并没有记载他的朋友是否接受了他的医疗帮助，但他很可能接受了。
en: History does not relate whether his friend accepted his medical help, but in all probability he did.

zh: 服药的最大好处在于，它对服药者没有任何要求，除了忍受片刻恶心的味道之外，这也是所有病人对医生的要求——在不给自己带来任何不便的情况下治愈疾病。
en: The great advantage of taking medicine is that it makes no demands on the taker beyond that of putting up for a moment with a disgusting taste,and that is what all patients demand of their doctors—to be cured at no inconvenience to themselves.

zh: 肯尼思·沃克 患者和医生
en: KENNETH WALKER Patients and doctors

zh: 本世纪已经开发出许多奇怪的新交通工具，其中最奇怪的也许是气垫船。
en: Many strange new means of transport have been developed in our century,the strangest of them being perhaps the hovercraft.

zh: 1953年，五十多岁的前电子工程师克里斯托弗·科克雷尔(Christopher Cockerell)转向诺福克湖区的造船业，向英国政府和工业界提出了他多年来一直致力于的一个想法。
en: In 1953,a former electronics engineer in his fifties,Christopher Cockerell,who had turned to boat-building on the Norfolk Broads,suggested an idea on which he had been working for many years to the British Government and industrial circles.

zh: 这个想法是将飞行器支撑在低压空气的“垫”或垫子上，周围环绕着高压空气幕。
en: It was the idea of supporting a craft on a 'pad’,or cushion,of low-pressure air,ringed with a curtain of higher pressure air.

zh: 从那时起，人们就很难决定这种飞行器应该属于轮船、飞机还是陆地车辆——因为它介于船和飞机之间。
en: Ever since,people have had difficulty in deciding whether the craft should be ranged among ships,planes,or land vehicles—for it is something in between a boat and an aircraft.

zh: 作为一名造船商，科克雷尔试图找到解决波浪阻力问题的方法，波浪阻力浪费了水面舰艇的大量动力并限制了其速度。
en: As a shipbuilder,Cockerell was trying to find a solution to the problem of the wave resistance which wastes a good deal of a surface ship's power and limits its speed.

zh: 他的答案是让船在不超过一两英尺厚的气垫上行驶，从而将船从水中提起。
en: His answer was to lift the vessel out of the water by making it ride on a cushion of air,no more than one or two feet thick.

zh: 这是通过飞行器底部的大量环形喷气口来完成的。
en: This is done by a great number of ring-shaped air jets on the bottom of the craft.

zh: 因此，它“飞”了，但它不能飞得更高——它的行动取决于它所骑过的表面、水或地面。
en: It ‘flies', therefore,but it cannot fly higher—its action depends on the surface,water or ground,over which it rides.

zh: 1959 年在索伦特海峡进行的首次测试引起了轰动。
en: The first tests on the Solent in 1959 caused a sensation.

zh: 气垫船首先在水面上航行，然后登上海滩，爬上沙丘，最后停在路上。
en: The hovercraft travelled first over the water,then mounted the beach,climbed up the dunes,and sat down on a road.

zh: 后来它横渡英吉利海峡，平稳地渡过海浪，没有出现任何问题。
en: Later it crossed the Channel,riding smoothly over the waves,which presented no problem.

zh: 从那时起，各种类型的气垫船出现并开始定期服役。
en: Since that time,various types of hovercraft have appeared and taken up regular service.

zh: 气垫船在非洲或澳大利亚等交通不便的大片地区特别有用；它可以成为“飞行的水果碗”，将香蕉从种植园运到港口；巨型气垫船班轮可以横跨大西洋；未来的铁路很可能是“气垫列车”，它依靠气垫行驶在一根从未接触过的铁轨上，速度高达 300 英里/小时——可能性似乎是无限的。
en: The hovercraft is particularly useful in large areas with poor communications such as Africa or Australia;it can become a 'flying fruit-bowl’,carrying bananas from the plantations to the ports;giant hovercraft liners could span the Atlantic;and the railway of the future may well be the ^hovertrain',riding on its air cushion over a single rail,which it never touches,at speeds up to 300 m.p.h.—the possibilities appear unlimited.

zh: 埃贡·拉森《飞马发明家之书》
en: EGON LARSEN The Pegasus Book of Inventors

zh: 一百年前，我们对海洋的了解仅限于海面的二维形状以及靠近陆地的浅水深度不规则所带来的航行危险。
en: Our knowledge of the oceans a hundred years ago was confined to the two-dimensional shape of the sea surface and the hazards of navigation presented by the irregularities in depth of the shallow water close to the land.

zh: 公海深邃而神秘，任何对海底有过深入思考的人都可能认为海床是平坦的。
en: The open sea was deep and mysterious,and anyone who gave more than a passing thought to the bottom confines of the oceans probably assumed that the sea bed was flat.

zh: 詹姆斯·克拉克·罗斯爵士 (Sir James Clark Ross) 在 1839 年测得了超过 2,400 英寻的水深，但直到 1869 年，H.
en: Sir James Clark Ross had obtained 5 a sounding of over 2,400 fathoms in 1839,but it was not until 1869,when H.

zh: 豪猪号曾多次被英国皇家学会使用，在大西洋进行了一系列深度探测，并通过疏浚海底收集了第一批样本。
en: Porcupine was put at the disposal of the Royal Society for several cruises,that a series of deep soundings was obtained in the Atlantic and the first samples were collected by dredging the bottom.

zh: 此后不久，著名的 H.M.S.
en: Shortly after this the famous H.

zh: 挑战者号探险队将海底研究确立为最有资格的物理学家和地质学家值得研究的课题。
en: Challenger expedition established the study of the sea-floor as a subject worthy of the most qualified physicists and geologists.

zh: 与海底电缆铺设相关的一系列活动很快证实了挑战者号的观察，即海洋的许多部分有两到三英里深，并且存在相当大的水下特征。
en: A burst of activity associated with the laying of submarine cables soon confirmed the Challenger's 10 observation that many parts of the ocean were two to three miles deep,and the existence of underwater features of considerable magnitude.

zh: 今天，有足够的探测资料可以绘制大西洋的地形图，并且我们对海底地形的多样性有所了解。
en: Today,enough soundings are available to enable a relief map of the Atlantic to be drawn and we know something of the great variety of the sea bed's topography.

zh: 由于海洋覆盖了地球表面的大部分，因此将海底视为地壳的基本形式，并叠加在其上的大陆、岛屿和海洋的其他特征是相当合理的。
en: Since the sea covers the greater part of the earth’s surface,it is quite reasonable to regard the sea floor as the basic form of the crust of the earth,with, 15 superimposed upon,it the continents,together with the islands and other features of the oceans.

zh: 这些大陆形成崎岖的台地，高出公海海底近三英里。
en: The continents form rugged tablelands which stand nearly three miles above the floor of the open ocean.

zh: 从海岸线到几英里到几百英里不等的地方，有大陆架的缓坡，在地质上是大陆的一部分。
en: From the shore line,out to a distance which may be anywhere from a few miles to a few hundred miles,runs the gentle slope of the continental shelf,geologically part of the continents.

zh: 大陆和海洋之间的真正分界线出现在更陡峭的斜坡脚下。
en: The real dividing line between continents and oceans occurs at the foot of a steeper slope.

zh: 这个大陆坡通常从接近 100 噚的地方开始，在几百英里的过程中到达真正的海底 2,500-3,500 噚。
en: This continental slope usually starts at a place somewhere near the 100-fathom mark and in the course of a few hundred miles reaches the true ocean floor at 2,500-3,500 fathoms.

zh: 坡度平均约为十分之一，但包含陡峭的、可能是垂直的悬崖和平缓的沉积物覆盖的阶地，在其下游附近有一个长期的尾部，这几乎可以肯定是物质被大陆块侵蚀后输送到深水的结果。
en: The slope averages about 1 in 30, but contains steep,probably vertical,cliffs,and gentle sediment-covered terraces,and near its lower reaches there is a long tailing-off which is almost certainly the result of material transported out to deep water after being eroded from the continental masses.

zh: 加斯克尔通过科学调查探索海底
en: GASKELL Exploring the Sea-floor from Science Survey

zh: 对雕塑的欣赏取决于对三个维度的形式做出反应的能力。
en: Appreciation of sculpture depends upon the ability to respond to form in three dimensions.

zh: 这也许就是为什么雕塑被描述为所有艺术中最难的；当然它比涉及平面形式、仅二维形状的艺术更难。
en: That is perhaps why sculpture has been described as the most difficult of all arts;certainly it is more difficult than the arts which involve appreciation of flat forms,shape in only two dimensions.

zh: “色盲”的人比“色盲”的人多得多。
en: Many more people are ‘form-blind' than colour-blind.

zh: 孩子在学习看东西时，首先只能辨别二维形状，无法判断距离、深度。
en: The child learning to see,first distinguishes only two-dimensional shape;it cannot judge distances,depths.

zh: 后来，出于人身安全和实际需要，它不得不发展（部分通过触摸）粗略判断三维距离的能力。
en: Later,for its personal safety and practical needs,it has to develop(partly by means of touch)the ability to judge roughly three-dimensonal distances.

zh: 但在满足了现实需要后，大多数人就不再继续了。
en: But having satisfied the requirements of practical necessity,most people go no further.

zh: 尽管他们可能在对平面形式的感知上获得相当大的准确性，但他们并没有做出进一步的智力和情感努力来理解形式的完整空间存在。
en: Though they may attain considerable accuracy in the perception of flat form,they do not make the further intellectual and emotionaleffort needed to comprehend form in its full spatial existence.

zh: 这是雕塑家必须做的。
en: This is what the sculptor must do.

zh: 他必须不断努力思考和使用形式的完整空间。
en: He must strive continually to think of,and use,form in its full spatial completeness.

zh: 可以说，他在他的脑袋里得到了实体的形状——他想到它，无论它有多大，就好像他把它完全封闭在他的手掌中一样。
en: He gets the solid shape,as it were,inside his head—he thinks of it,whatever its size,as if he were holding it completely enclosed in the hollow of his hand.

zh: 他在心里想象出一个复杂的形状，从四周看去；当他看一侧时，他知道另一侧是什么样子；他用它的重心、质量、重量来识别自己；他认识到它的体积，作为形状在空气中移动的空间。
en: He mentally visualizes a complex form from all round itself;he knows while he looks at one side what the other side is like;he identifies himself with its centre of gravity,its mass,its weight;he realizes its volume,as the space that the shape displaces in the air.

zh: 敏感的雕塑观察者也必须学会将形状简单地当作形状来感受，而不是作为描述或回忆。
en: And the sensitive observer of sculpture must also learn to feel shape simply as shape,not as description or reminiscence.

zh: 例如，他必须将鸡蛋视为一个简单的单一固体形状，这与它作为食物的意义，或者与它会变成一只鸟的文学观念完全不同。
en: He must,for example,perceive an egg as a simple single solid shape,quite apart from its significance as food,or from the literary idea that it will become a bird.

zh: 贝壳、坚果、李子、梨、蝌蚪、蘑菇、山峰、肾脏、胡萝卜、树干、鸟、花蕾、云雀、瓢虫、芦苇、骨头等固体也是如此。
en: And so with solids such as a shell, a nut,a plum,a pear,a tadpole,a mushroom,a mountain peak,a kidney,a carrot,a tree-trunk,a bird,a bud, a lark,a ladybird,a bulrush,a bone.

zh: 由此，他可以继续欣赏更复杂的形式或几种形式的组合。
en: From these he can go on to appreciate more complex forms or combinations of several forms.

zh: 雕塑家亨利·摩尔 (HENRY MOORE) 的《倾听者》
en: HENRY MOORE The Sculptor Speaks from The Listener

zh: 伽利略在世时曾是激烈争议的中心；但科学尘埃早已尘埃落定，今天我们甚至可以从正确的角度看待他与宗教裁判所的著名冲突。
en: In his own lifetime Galileo was the centre of violent controversy;but the scientific dust has long since settled,and today we can see even his famous clash with the Inquisition in something like its proper perspective.

zh: 但相比之下，直到近代，伽利略才成为科学史学家的问题儿童。
en: But,in contrast,it is only in modern times that Galileo has become a problem child for historians of science.

zh: 伽利略的旧观点非常简单。
en: The old view of Galileo was delightfully uncomplicated.

zh: 首先，他是一个勇于实验的人：他鄙视亚里士多德学派的偏见和书本知识，他向自然而不是古人提出问题，并且无所畏惧地得出结论。
en: He was,above all,a man who experimented: who despised the prejudices and book learning of the Aristotelians,who put his questions to nature instead of to the ancients,and who drew his conclusions fearlessly.

zh: 他是第一个将望远镜转向天空的人，他所看到的证据足以推翻亚里士多德和托勒密。
en: He had been the first to turn a telescope to the sky,and he had seen there evidence enough to overthrow Aristotle and Ptolemy together.

zh: 他是那个爬上比萨斜塔并从塔顶落下各种重物、让球滚下斜面，然后将他的许多实验结果推广到著名的自由落体定律的人。
en: He was the man who climbed the Leaning Tower of Pisa and dropped various weights from the top,who rolled balls down inclined planes,and then generalized the results of his many experiments into the famous law of free fall.

zh: 但是，对证据的更仔细的研究，以及对那个时代的更深刻的认识，特别是对科学革命中哲学暗流的新意识的支持，已经深刻地改变了伽利略的这一观点。
en: But a closer study of the evidence,supported by a deeper sense of the period,and particularly by a new consciousness of the philosophical undercurrents in the scientific revolution,has profoundly modified this view of Galileo.

zh: 如今，尽管古老的伽利略仍然存在于许多流行著作中，但科学史学家们却出现了一幅新的、更复杂的图景。
en: Today,although the old Galileo lives on in many popular writings,among historians of science a new and more sophisticated picture has emerged.

zh: 与此同时，我们对伽利略的反对者的同情有所增加。
en: At the same time our sympathy for Galileo's opponents has grown somewhat.

zh: 他的望远镜观测是不朽的；它们在当时引起了极大的兴趣，产生了重要的理论后果，并为仪器和设备中隐藏的潜力提供了惊人的展示。
en: His telescopic observations are justly immortal;they aroused great interest at the time,they had important theoretical consequences,and they provided a striking demonstration of the potentialities hidden in instruments and apparatus.

zh: 但是，如果我们记得使用望远镜的极限能力需要长期的经验和对仪器的熟悉，我们是否可以责怪那些看过但未能看到伽利略所看到的东西的人呢？
en: But can we blame those who looked and failed to see what Galileo saw,if we remember that to use a telescope at the limit of its powers calls for long experience and intimate familiarity with one's instrument?

zh: 拒绝通过伽利略望远镜观察的哲学家是否比那些声称十八世纪四十年代用罗塞勋爵的大望远镜观察到的螺旋星云是研磨机留下的划痕的哲学家更有罪？
en: Was the philosopher who refused to look through Galileo's telescope more culpable than those who alleged that the spiral nebulae observed with Lord Rosse's great telescope in the eighteen-forties were scratches left by the grinder?

zh: 如果我们回想一下，在他那个时代，就像几个世纪之前一样，曲面玻璃是一种流行的发明，它不是产生真理，而是产生幻觉和谎言；如果单个曲面玻璃会扭曲自然，那么一对曲面玻璃会扭曲多少呢？
en: We can perhaps forgive those who said the moons of Jupiter were produced by Galileo's spyglass if we recall that in his day,as for centuries before, curved glass was the popular contrivance for producing not truth but illusion,untruth;and if a single curved glass would distort nature,how much more would a pair of them?

zh: 迈克尔·霍金 伽利略重生，来自《倾听者》
en: MICHAELHoSKIN Galileo Reborn from The Listener

zh: 教育是我们这个时代的关键词之一。
en: Education is one of the key words of our time.

zh: 我们许多人认为，一个没有受过教育的人是不利环境的不幸受害者，被剥夺了二十世纪最伟大的机会之一。
en: A man without an education,many of us believe,is an unfortunate victim of adverse circumstances,deprived of one of the greatest twentieth-century opportunities.

zh: 现代国家深信教育的重要性，对学习机构进行“投资”，以培养大批开明的青年男女，他们是潜在的领导者，以重新获得“兴趣”。
en: Convinced of the importance of education,modern states ‘invest'in institutions of learning to get back 'interest'in the form of a large group of enlightened young men and women who are potential leaders.

zh: 教育，其教学周期如此精心设计，并以教科书为间断——那些可以购买的智慧之井——如果没有它的好处，文明会是什么样子？
en: Education,with its cycles of instruction so carefully worked out,punctuated by textbooks—those purchasable wells of wisdom—what would civilization be like without its benefits?

zh: 可以肯定的是：我们会有医生和传教士、律师和被告、婚姻和生育——但我们的精神面貌会有所不同。
en: So much is certain:that we would have doctors and preachers,lawyers and defendants,marriages and births—but our spiritual outlook would be different.

zh: 我们将更少地强调“事实和数据”，而更多地强调良好的记忆力、应用心理学以及一个人与同胞相处的能力。
en: We would lay less stress on 'facts and figures'and more on a good memory,on applied psychology,and on the capacity of a man to get along with his fellow- citizens.

zh: 如果我们的教育体系是按照过去无书本的方式建立的，我们就会拥有可以想象到的最民主的“大学”形式。
en: If our educational system were fashioned after its bookless past we would have the most democratic form of ‘college'imaginable.

zh: 在部落人民中，所有传统继承的知识都是所有人共享的；它被传授给部落的每个成员，以便在这方面每个人都平等地为生活做好准备。
en: Among tribal people all knowledge inherited by tradition is shared by all;it is taught to every member of the tribe so that in this respect everybody is equally equipped for life.

zh: 这是“平等起点”的理想条件，只有我们最先进的现代教育形式才能试图重新获得这种条件。
en: It is the ideal condition of the ‘equal start’which only our most progressive forms of modern education try to regain.

zh: 在原始文化中，寻求和接受传统指导的义务对所有人都有约束力。
en: In primitive cultures the obligation to seek and to receive the traditional instruction is binding to all.

zh: 不存在“文盲”——如果这个词可以适用于没有文字的民族的话——而我们自己的义务教育制度在德国于 1642 年、在法国于 1806 年、在英国于 1876 年成为法律，并且在许多“文明”国家中仍然不存在。
en: There are no 'illiterates'—if the term can be applied to peoples without a script—while our own compulsory school attendance became law in Germany in 1642,in France in 1806,and in England in 1876, and is still non-existent in a number of 'civilized'nations.

zh: 这表明我们花了多长时间才认为有必要确保我们所有的孩子都能分享过去几个世纪中幸福的少数人积累的知识。
