en: This shows how long it was before we deemed it necessary to make sure that all our children could share in the knowledge accumulated by the happy few' during the past centuries.

zh: 荒野教育不是金钱问题。
en: Education in the wilderness is not a matter of monetary means.

zh: 所有人都有权享有平等的起点。
en: All are entitled to an equal start.

zh: 在我们的社会中，不存在常常妨碍成长中个性的充分发展的匆忙。
en: There is none of the hurry which,in our society,often hampers the full development of a growing personality.

zh: 在那里，孩子在父母无时无刻的关注下长大；因此丛林和大草原上不存在“青少年犯罪”。
en: There, a child grows up under the ever-present attention of his parents;therefore the jungles and the savannahs know of no ‘juvenile delinquency'.

zh: 离家谋生的必要性不会导致对孩子的忽视，也没有哪个父亲会面临自己无力为孩子“花钱”接受教育的情况。
en: No necessity of making a living away from home results in neglect of children,and no father is confronted with his inability to 'buy'an education for his child.

zh: 朱利叶斯·E.
en: JULIUS E.

zh: 嘴唇 事物的起源
en: LIPS The Origin of Things

zh: 这几乎总是让青少年感到震惊，并让他们下定决心，今后不再与父母谈论他们去过的地方或人。
en: 5 adolescents,and makes them resolve that in future they will not talk to their parents about the places or people they visit.

zh: 不久之后，父母就会抱怨孩子太守口如瓶，从不告诉他们任何事情，但他们很少意识到这是他们自己造成的。
en: Before very long the parents will be complaining that the child is so secretive and never tells them anything,but they seldom realize that they have brought this on themselves.

zh: 对父母的幻想破灭，无论他们作为父母和个人多么优秀和足够，在某种程度上都是不可避免的。
en: Disillusionment with the parents,however good and adequate they may be both as parents and as individuals,is to some degree inevitable.

zh: 大多数孩子对父母抱有如此高的理想，除非父母本身一直不如人意，否则很难指望经得起现实的评价。
en: Most children have such a high ideal of their parents,unless the 10 parents themselves have been unsatisfactory,that it can hardly hope to stand up to a realistic evaluation.

zh: 如果父母意识到他们的孩子通常对自己的品格和正确性抱有多大的信念，以及这种信念对孩子的意义有多大，他们会感到非常惊讶和深深的感动。
en: Parents would be greatly surprised and deeply touched if they realized how much belief their children usually have in their character and infallibility,and how much this faith means to a child.

zh: 如果父母对青少年的这种反应有所准备，并认识到这是孩子成长并发展宝贵的观察力和独立判断力的标志，他们就不会受到如此伤害，因此也不会通过怨恨和抵制来驱使孩子反对。
en: If parents were prepared for this adolescent reaction,and realized that it was a sign that the child was growing up and developing valuable powers of observation and independent judgment,they would not be so hurt,and 15 therefore would not drive the child into opposition by resenting and resisting it.

zh: 青少年热衷于真诚，总是尊重承认自己错误、无知、甚至不公平或不公正的父母。
en: The adolescent,with his passion for sincerity,always respects a parent who admits that he is wrong,or ignorant,or even that he has been unfair or unjust.

zh: 孩子无法原谅的是，如果孩子知道这些指控是真的，父母却拒绝承认这些指控。
en: What the child cannot forgive is the parents'refusal to admit these charges if the child knows them to be true.

zh: 维多利亚时代的父母认为，他们通过退却不合理的独裁态度来维护自己的尊严；事实上，他们并没有做这样的事情，但孩子们太害怕了，不敢让他们知道自己的真实感受。
en: Victorian parents believed that they kept their dignity by retreating behind an unreasoning authoritarian 20 attitude;in fact they did nothing of the kind,but children were then too cowed to let them know how they really felt.

zh: 今天我们倾向于走向另一个极端，但总的来说，这对孩子和父母来说都是更健康的态度。
en: Today we tend to go to the other extreme,but on the whole this is a healthier attitude both for the child and the parent.

zh: 面对现实总是更明智、更安全的做法，无论此刻有多痛苦。
en: It is always wiser and safer to face up to reality,however painful it may be at the moment.

zh: DoRIS ODLUM 青春期之旅
en: DoRIS ODLUM Journey Through Adolescence

zh: 月球很可能成为太阳系的工业中心，为其飞船提供火箭燃料，这些燃料很容易从月球岩石中以液氧的形式获得。
en: The Moon is likely to become the industrial hub of the Solar System,supplying the rocket fuels for its ships, easily obtainable from the lunar rocks in the form of liquid oxygen.

zh: 原因就在于它的重力。
en: The reason lies in its gravity.

zh: 由于月球的质量只有地球的八十分之一，因此从月球到地球轨道行驶 25 万英里所需的能量比从地球表面进入轨道 200 英里所需的能量少 97%！
en: Because the Moon has only an eightieth of the Earth's mass,it requires 97 per cent less energy to travel the quarter of a million miles from the Moon to Earth-orbit than the 200 mile-journey from Earth's surface into orbit!

zh: 这听起来可能很不可思议，但很容易计算。
en: This may sound fantastic,but it is easily calculated.

zh: 要乘坐火箭逃离地球，必须以每秒七英里的速度行驶。
en: To escape from the Earth in a rocket,one must travel at seven miles per second.

zh: 从月球出发的可比速度仅为每秒 1.5 英里。
en: The comparable speed from the Moon is only 1.5 miles per second.

zh: 由于月球表面的重力仅为地球的六分之一（请记住阿波罗宇航员是多么容易地跳跃），因此加速到每秒 1.5 英里所需的能量比在地球上要少得多。
en: Because the gravity on the Moon's surface is only a sixth of Earth's (remember how easily the Apollo astronauts bounded along),it takes much less energy to accelerate to that 1.5 miles per second than it does on Earth.

zh: 月球居民将能够在太空中飞行，而成本仅为其陆地表亲类似旅行的百分之三。
en: Moon- dwellers willbe able to fly in space at only three per cent of the cost of similar journeys by their terrestrial cousins.

zh: 阿瑟·C.
en: Arthur C.

zh: 克拉克曾经建议，一个革命性的想法要经历三个阶段：“这是不可能的——别浪费我的时间。”
en: Clark once suggested a revolutionary idea passes through three phases: 'It's impossible—don't waste my time.

zh: ’这是可能的，但不值得做。
en: ' It's possible,but not worth doing.

zh: ” 3 “我说过这一直都是个好主意。
en: ' 3 'I said it was a good idea all along.

zh: 殖民火星——一个比月球远160倍的世界——的想法将从第二阶段决定性地转向第三阶段，届时将有大量的人永久生活在太空中。
en: ' The idea of colonising Mars—a world 160 times more distant than the Moon—will move decisively from the second phase to the third,when a significant number of people are living permanently in space.

zh: 火星对未来的航行者有着非凡的吸引力。
en: Mars has an extraordinary fascination for would-be voyagers.

zh: 美国、俄罗斯和欧洲充满了热衷者，其中许多人是认真的资深科学家，他们梦想着将人们送往那里。
en: America,Russia and Europe are filled with enthusiasts—many of them serious and senior scientists—who dream of sending people to it.

zh: 他们的目的是可以理解的。
en: Their aim is understandable.

zh: 它是太阳系中最像地球的一个世界。
en: It is the one world in the Solar System that is most like the Earth.

zh: 这是一个充满红色沙漠（因此得名“红色星球”）、万里无云的天空、狂暴的沙尘暴、比大峡谷更宽的峡谷和至少一座比珠穆朗玛峰高两倍以上的山峰的世界。
en: It is a world of red sandy deserts (hence its name —the Red Planet),cloudless skies,savage sandstorms,chasms wider than the Grand Canyon and at least one mountain more than twice as tall as Everest.

zh: 这似乎是解决问题的理想选择。 1989年2月19日7天
en: It seems ideal for settlement. 7DAYS,February 19,1989

zh: 如果一个国家本质上不团结，那就由政府来维持它。
en: If a nation is essentially disunited,it is left to the government to hold it together.

zh: 这增加了政府的开支，相应地减少了可用于国家发展的经济资源。
en: This increases the expense of government,and reduces correspondingly the amount of economic resources that could be used for developing the country.

zh: 我们不应该忘记，在一个贫穷落后的国家，这些资源是多么的少。
en: And it should not be forgotten how small those resources are in a poor and backward country.

zh: 政府成本高的地方，发展资源相应就低。
en: Where the cost of government is high,resources for development are correspondingly low.

zh: 这可以通过将一个国家的地位与一个私营企业的地位进行比较来说明。
en: This may be ilustrated by comparing the position of a nation with that of a private business enterprise.

zh: 企业为了维持经营，必须承担一定的成本和费用。
en: An enterprise has to incur certain costs and expenses in order to stay in business.

zh: 就我们的目的而言，我们只关心一种成本——管理和管理业务的成本。
en: For our purposes,we are concerned only with one kind of cost--the cost of managing and administering the business.

zh: 企业的此类行政管理费用类似于一个国家的政府成本。
en: Such administrative overheads in a business are analogous to the cost of government in a nation.

zh: 企业的管理费用很低，以至于可以相信在企业中工作的每个人都会以最有利于公司利益的方式行事。
en: The administrative overheads of a business are low to the extent that everyone working in the business can be trusted to behave 10 in a way that best promotes the interests of the firm.

zh: 如果可以相信他们每个人都能承担这样的责任，并在自己的职责范围内发挥主动性，那么行政管理费用就会很低。
en: If they can each be trusted to take such responsibilities, and to exercise such initiative as falls within their sphere,then administrative overheads will be low.

zh: 它会很低，因为每项工作只需要一个人来负责，而不需要另一个人来检查他正在做的事情，让他保持一致，并向其他人报告他的情况。
en: It will be low because it willbe necessary to have only one man looking after each job,without having another man to check upon what he is doing,keep him in line,and report on him to someone else.

zh: 但如果没有人可以被信任以忠诚和负责任的方式对待他的工作，那么企业将需要大量的管理员、检查员和领班，管理费用也会相应增加。
en: But if no one can be trusted to act in a loyal and responsible manner towards his job,then the business will require armies of administrators,checkers,and foremen,and administrative overheads will rise correspondingly.

zh: 随着管理费用的增加，企业在支付管理费用后的收益将会下降；企业用于分配股息或直接投资于其未来进步和发展的资金将会减少。
en: As administrative overheads rise,so the earnings of the business after meeting the expense of administration, willfall;and the business will have less money to distribute as dividends or invest directly in its future progress and development.

zh: 对于一个民族来说也是如此。
en: It is precisely the same with a nation.

zh: 如果可以信赖人民以忠诚和负责任的方式行事，政府就不需要警察和公务员军队来维持秩序。
en: To the extent that the people can be relied upon to behave in a loyal 20 and responsible manner,the government does not require armies of police and civil servants to keep them in order.

zh: 但如果一个国家不团结，政府就不能确定人民的行为是否符合国家的利益，而必须相应地监视、检查和控制人民。
en: But if a nation is disunited,the government cannot be sure that the actions of the people will be in the interests of the nation;and it will have to watch,check,and control the people accordingly.

zh: 因此，一个分裂的国家必须承担过高的政府成本。
en: A disunited nation therefore has to incur unduly high costs of government.

zh: 雷蒙德·弗罗斯特 落后社会
en: RAYMOND FROST The Backward Society

zh: 十二岁是人体最旺盛的时期。
en: At the age of twelve years,the human body is at its most vigorous.

zh: 它还没有达到其最大的尺寸和力量，其主人也尚未达到其全部的智力；但在这个年龄，死亡的可能性是最小的。
en: It has yet to reach its full size and strength,and its owner his or her full intelligence;but at this age the likelihood of death is least.

zh: 早些时候，我们还是婴儿和幼儿，因此更加脆弱；后来，我们将经历活力和抵抗力的逐渐丧失，尽管一开始难以察觉，但最终将变得如此严重，以至于我们无法再活下去，无论我们如何照顾自己，无论社会和医生如何照顾我们。
en: Earlier,we were infants and young children,and consequently more vulnerable;later,we shall undergo a progressive loss of our vigour and resistance which,though imperceptible at first,will finally become so steep that we 5 can live no longer,however wellwe look after ourselves,and however well society,and our doctors,look after us.

zh: 这种活力随着时间的流逝而下降的现象称为衰老。
en: This decline in vigour with the passing of time is called ageing.

zh: 我们所有人都做出的最令人不快的发现之一是，我们必须以这种方式衰落，如果我们逃避战争、事故和疾病，我们最终将“死于老年”，而且这种情况发生的速度因人而异，因此，我们在六十五岁到八十岁之间死亡的可能性很大。我们中的一些人会死得更快，一些人会活得更久——活到第九个或第十个十年。
en: It is one of the most.unpleasant discoveries which we all make that we must decline in this way,that if we escape wars,accidents and diseases we shall eventually 'die of old age',and that this happens at a rate which differs little from person to person,so that there are heavy odds in favour of our dying between the ages of sixty-five and eighty. 10 Some of us will die sooner,a few will live longer—on into a ninth or tenth decade.

zh: 但机会渺茫，无论我们多么幸运和坚强，我们能活多久实际上是有限的。
en: But the chances are against it,and there is a virtual limit on how long we can hope to remain alive,however lucky and robust we are.

zh: 普通人往往会忘记这个过程，除非他们被提醒。
en: Normal people tend to forget this process unless and until they are reminded of it.

zh: 我们对人会衰老的事实如此熟悉，多年来人们一直认为随着时间的流逝而失去活力，随着年龄的增长而更有可能死亡的过程是不言而喻的，就像热水壶的冷却或一双鞋子的磨损一样。
en: We are sofamiliar with the fact that man ages,that people have for years assumed that the process of losing vigour with time, 15 of becoming more likely to die the older we get,was something self-evident,like the cooling of a hot kettle or the wearing-out of a pair of shoes.

zh: 他们还假设所有动物，可能还有树木等其他有机体，甚至宇宙本身，本质上都必须“磨损”。
en: They have also assumed that all animals,and probably other organisms such as trees,or even the universe itself,must in the nature of things‘wear out'.

zh: 如果有机会活得足够长，我们通常观察到的大多数动物实际上都会像我们一样衰老；而像上链手表或太阳这样的机械系统，实际上会根据热力学第二定律 20 耗尽能量（目前整个宇宙是否会这样做还是一个有争议的问题）。
en: Most animals we commonly observe do in fact age as we do,if given the chance to live long enough;and mechanical systems like a wound watch,or the sun,do in fact run out of energy in accordance with the second law of thermodynamics 20(whether the whole universe does so is a moot point at present).

zh: 但这些与人类衰老时发生的情况不同。
en: But these are not analogous to what happens when man ages.

zh: 破旧的手表仍然是手表，可以重新上发条。
en: A run-down watch is still a watch and can be rewound.

zh: 相比之下，一块旧手表会变得非常磨损且不可靠，最终不值得修理。
en: An old watch,by contrast,becomes so worn and unreliable that it eventually is not worth mending.

zh: 但手表永远无法自我修复——它不包含活动部件，仅由金属组成，会因摩擦而磨损。
en: But a watch could never repair itself—it does not consist of living parts,only of metal,which wears away by friction.

zh: 我们可以一次修复自己——至少足以克服除最直接致命的疾病和事故之外的所有疾病和事故。 25 在十二岁到八十岁之间，我们逐渐失去了这种力量；十二岁时，疾病会将我们击倒，八十岁时，疾病会将我们击倒，带入坟墓。
en: We could,at one time,repair ourselves—well enough,at least,to overcome all but the most instantly fatal illnesses and accidents. 25 Between twelve and eighty years we gradually lose this power;an llnesswhich at twelve would knock us over,at eighty can knock us out,and into our grave.

zh: 如果我们能像十二岁时那样精力充沛，大约需要700年，我们就会有一半的人死亡，而另外700年，幸存者又会减少一半。
en: If we could stay as vigorous as we are at twelve,itwould take about 700 years for half of us to die,and another 700 for the survivors to be reduced by half again.

zh: ALEX CoMFORT 衰老的过程
en: ALEx CoMFORT The process of ageing

zh: 供水污染通常是由于水源附近的卫生条件差、污水排入水源本身、污水泄漏到分配系统或工业或农业废物污染造成的。
en: Contamination of water supplies is usually due to poor sanitation close to water sources,sewage disposal into the sources themselves,leakage of sewage into distribution systems or contamination with industrial or farm waste.

zh: 即使管道供水在其源头是安全的，但到达水龙头时并不总是安全的。
en: Even if a piped water supply is safe at its source,it is not always safe by the time it reaches the tap.

zh: 间歇性的自来水供应尤其值得怀疑。
en: Intermittent tap-water supplies should be regarded as particularly suspect.

zh: 前往供水质量不稳定地区短途旅行的旅行者应避免饮用自来水或任何其他来源的未经处理的水。
en: Travellers on short trips to areas with water supplies of uncertain quality should avoid drinking tap- water,or untreated water from any other source.

zh: 最好只喝热饮、知名品牌的瓶装或罐装饮料——装瓶厂通常遵循国际水处理标准。
en: It is best to keep to hot drinks,bottled or canned drinks of well-known brand names—international standards of water treatment are usually followed at bottling plants.

zh: 碳酸饮料是酸性的，稍微安全一些。
en: Carbonated drinks are acidic,and slightly safer.

zh: 确保所有瓶子都在您在场的情况下打开，并且其边缘干净且干燥。
en: Make sure that all bottles are opened in your presence,and that their rims are clean and dry.

zh: 煮沸始终是处理水的好方法。
en: Boiling is always a good way of treating water.

zh: 一些酒店可应要求提供开水，可用于饮用或刷牙。
en: Some hotels supply boiled water on request and this can be used for drinking,or for brushing teeth.

zh: 当电压合适时，可以煮沸少量水的便携式煮沸元件非常有用。
en: Portable boiling elements that can boil small quantities of water are useful when the right voltage of electricity is available.

zh: 礼貌地拒绝任何来源不明的冷饮。
en: Refuse politely any cold drink from an unknown source.

zh: 冰的安全性取决于其所用的水，除非已知冰是安全的，否则不应将其放入饮料中。
en: Ice is only as safe as the water from which it is made,and should not be put in drinks unless it is known to be safe.

zh: 饮料可以通过将其放在冰上而不是加冰来冷却。
en: Drinks can be cooled by placing them on ice rather than adding ice to them.

zh: 酒精可能是一种医用消毒剂，但不能用来对水进行消毒。
en: Alcohol may be a medical disinfectant,but should not be relied upon to sterilize water.

zh: 乙醇浓度在50-70%时效果较好；低于1%时，其杀菌作用可以忽略不计。
en: Ethanol is more effective at a concentration of 50-70 per cent;below 20 per cent,its bactericidal action is negligible.

zh: 标有 95 度的烈酒仅含有约 47% 的酒精度。
en: Spirits labelled 95 proof contain only about 47 per cent alcohol.

zh: 谨防甲基化酒精，它的毒性非常大，切勿将其添加到饮用水中。
en: Beware of methylated alcohol,which is very poisonous,and should never be added to drinking water.

zh: 如果无法获得其他安全供水，可以将太热而无法触摸的自来水冷却，通常可以安全饮用。
en: If no other safe water supply can be obtained,tap water that is too hot to touch can be left to cool and is generally safe to drink.

zh: 那些计划前往偏远地区旅行或打算居住在饮用水缺乏的国家的人应该了解确保水安全的各种可能方法。
en: Those planning a trip to remote areas,or intending to live in countries where drinking water is not readily available,should know about the various possible methods for making water safe.

zh: 理查德·达伍德 旅行者健康
en: RICHARD DAWOOD Travellers'Health

zh: 我认识的作家很少，但那些我认识的、我尊敬的作家立刻承认，当他们第一次动笔写字时，他们几乎不知道自己要去哪里。
en: I have known very few writers,but those I have known,and whom I respect,confess at once that they have little idea where they are going when they first set pen to paper.

zh: 他们有一个性格，也许有两个；他们处于那种被视为灵感的急切不适的状态；一旦旅程开始，所有人都承认目的地发生了根本性的变化；据我所知，其中一个花了九个月的时间写了一本关于克什米尔的小说，然后在苏格兰高地重置了整个事情。
en: They have a character,perhaps two;they are in that condition of eager discomfort which passes for inspiration;all admit radical changes of destination once the journey has begun;one,to my certain knowledge,spent nine months on a novel about Kashmir, then reset the whole thing in the Scottish Highlands.

zh: 我从来没有听说过有人制作“骨架”，正如我们在学校所教的那样。
en: I never heard of anyone making a 'skeleton’,as we were taught at school.

zh: 在打破和重塑中，在时机、交织和重新开始中，作家开始在材料中辨别出那些在他开始时没有意识到的东西。
en: In the breaking and remaking,in the timing,interweaving,beginning afresh,the writer comes to discern things in his material which were not consciously in his mind when he began.

zh: 这种有机的过程往往会带来非凡的自我发现时刻，具有难以形容的魅力。
en: This organic process,often leading to moments of extraordinary self-discovery,is of an indescribable fascination.

zh: 一个模糊的图像出现了；他加上了一笔又一笔，它就消失了；但有一些东西在那里，他不会休息，直到他捕捉到它。
en: A blurred image appears;he adds a brushstroke and another,and it is gone;but something was there,and he will not rest till he has captured it.

zh: 有时，作家内心的酵母比他写的书还要长久。
en: Sometimes the yeast within a writer outlives a book he has written.

zh: 我听说有些作家只读自己的书，就像青少年一样站在镜子前，仍然无法理解眼前景象的确切轮廓。
en: I have heard of writers who read nothing but their own books;like adolescents they stand before the mirror,and still cannot fathom the exact outline of the vision before them.

zh: 出于同样的原因，作家们没完没了地谈论自己的书，揭示隐藏的含义，叠加新的含义，乞求周围人的回应。
en: For the same reason,writers talk interminably about their own books,winkling out hidden meanings,super-imposing new ones,begging response from those around them.

zh: 当然，这样做的作家会被误解：他还不如尝试解释犯罪或爱情。
en: Of course a writer doing this is misunderstood:he might as well try to explain a crime or a love affair.

zh: 顺便说一句，他也是一个不可原谅的讨厌鬼。
en: He is also,incidentally,an unforgivable bore.

zh: 这种拉近自己与读者之间的距离、研究他在那些不认识他的人眼中的形象的诱惑，可能会导致他的毁灭：他已经开始为了取悦而写作。
en: This temptation to cover the distance between himself and the reader,to study his image in the sight of those who do not know him,can be his undoing:he has begun to write to please.

zh: 一位年轻的英国作家在一两年前做出了相关的观察：才华进入初稿，艺术进入随后的草稿。
en: A young English writer made the pertinent observation a year or two back that the talent goes into the first draft,and the art into the drafts that follow.

zh: 因此，作家也像其他艺术家一样，没有休息的地方，没有人群或运动可以让他感到安慰，没有外部的判断可以取代内部的判断。
en: For this reason also the writer,like any other artist,has no resting place,no crowd or movement in which he may take comfort,no judgment from outside which can replace the judgment from within.

zh: 作家从内心的无政府状态中恢复秩序；他让自己服从比任何批评家所梦想的更无情的纪律，当他与名声调情时，他就从与自己相处的时间中抽出时间，从寻找他的世界最深处所包含的东西中抽出时间。
en: A writer makes order out of the anarchy of his heart;he submits himself to a more ruthless discipline than any critic dreamed of,and when he flirts with fame,he is taking time off from living with himself,from the search for what his world contains at its inmost point.

zh: 约翰·勒·卡雷 (John LE CARRÉ) 每个作家都想从《哈珀》杂志获得什么
en: JoHN LE CARRÉ What every writer wants from Harper's

zh: 波浪是海洋和大气之间斗争的产物，是无限的持续特征。
en: Waves are the children of the struggle between ocean and atmosphere,the ongoing signatures of infinity.

zh: 来自太阳的光线激发并激发了地球的大气层，唤醒它流动、运动、节奏和生命。
en: Rays from the sun excite and energize the atmosphere of the earth,awakening it to flow,to movement,to rhythm,to life.

zh: 然后，风向大海传达太阳的信息，而大海则通过波浪将其传递——这是一个古老、精致、有力的信息。
en: The wind then speaks the message of the sun to the sea and the sea transmits it on through waves—an ancient,exquisite,powerful message.

zh: 这些海浪是地球上最复杂的自然现象之一。
en: These ocean waves are among the earth's most complicated natural phenomena.

zh: 基本特征包括波峰（波的最高点）、波谷（最低点）、高度（从波谷到波峰的垂直距离）、波长（两个波峰之间的水平距离）和周期（波峰传播一个波长所需的时间）。
en: The basic features include a crest(the highest point of the wave),a trough(the lowest point),a height(the vertical distance from the trough to the crest),a wave length(the horizontal distance between two wave crests),and a period (which is the time it takes a wave crest to travel one wave length).

zh: 尽管海浪给人的印象是一堵水墙朝您的方向移动，但实际上，海浪在水中移动，使水保持在原来的位置。
en: Although an ocean wave gives the impression of a wallof water moving in your direction,in actuality 10 waves move through the water leaving the water about where it was.

zh: 如果水随波浪移动，海洋和海洋上的一切都会冲向海岸，造成明显的灾难性后果。
en: If the water was moving with the wave, the ocean and everything on it would be racing in to the shore with obviously catastrophic results.

zh: 穿过深水的海浪导致表面上的粒子沿着大致圆形的轨道移动，首先将粒子拉向前进的波浪，然后向上进入波浪，然后与波浪一起前进，然后——当波浪将粒子抛在后面时——再次回到其起点。
en: An ocean wave passing through deep water causes a particle on the surface to move in a roughly circular orbit,drawing the particle first towards the advancing wave,then up into the wave,then forward with it and then—as the wave leaves the particles behind—back to its starting point again.

zh: 从成熟到死亡，波浪与任何其他生物都遵循相同的法则。
en: From both maturity to death,a wave is subject to the same laws as any other living’thing.

zh: 有一段时间，它呈现出一种神奇的个性，最终被重新吸收到生命的浩瀚海洋中。
en: For a time it assumes a miraculous individuality that,in the end,is reabsorbed into the great ocean of life.

zh: 公海起伏的波浪是由三种自然原因产生的：风、地球运动或震动、以及月球和太阳的引力。
en: The undulating waves of the open sea are generated by three natural causes:wind,earth movements or tremors,and the gravitational pull of the moon and the sun.

zh: 一旦波浪产生，重力就会驱动它们不断尝试将海洋表面恢复为平坦的平原。来自世界杂志（BBC Enterprises）
en: Once waves have been generated,gravity is the force that drives them in a continual attempt to restore the ocean surface to a flat plain. from World Magazine(BBC Enterprises)

zh: 训练大象主要使用两种技术，我们可以分别称之为“强硬”和“温和”。
en: Two main techniques have been used for training elephants,which we may call respectively the tough and the gentle.

zh: 前一种方法只是让大象工作并殴打它，直到它完成预期的任务。
en: The former method simply consists of setting an elephant to work and beating him until he does what is expected of him.

zh: 撇开任何道德考虑不谈，这是一种愚蠢的训练方法，因为它会产生一种怨恨的动物，而这些动物在后期很可能会变成杀人犯。
en: Apart from any moral considerations this is a stupid method of training,for it produces a resentful animal who at a later stage may well turn man-killer.

zh: 温和的方法在早期阶段需要更多的耐心，但会产生一头开朗、脾气好的大象，它将提供多年的忠诚服务。
en: The gentle method requires more 5 patience in the early stages,but produces a cheerful,good-tempered elephant who will give many years of loyal service.

zh: 大象训练的首要任务是为大象指派一名驯象师，他将完全负责这项工作。
en: The first essential in elephant training is to assign to the animal a single mahout who will be entirely responsible for the job.

zh: 大象和狗一样喜欢有一个主人，并且有相当程度的个人感情。
en: Elephants like to have one master just as dogs do,and are capable of a considerable degree of personal affection.

zh: 甚至有这样的故事：未经训练的小象由于某种不可避免的情况而被剥夺了自己的训练师，因此拒绝进食并被活活饿死。
en: There are even stories of half-trained elephant calves who have refused to feed 10 and pined to death when by some unavoidable circumstance they have been deprived of their own trainer.

zh: 对于这样的极端案例，我们可能必须持保留态度，但它们确实强调了一个普遍原则：大象和驯象师之间的关系是成功训练的关键。
en: Such extreme cases must probably be taken with a grain of salt,but they do underline the general principle that the relationship between elephant and mahout is the key to successful training.

zh: 捕获大象进行训练最经济的年龄是十五岁到二十岁之间，因为此时大象几乎已经准备好承担繁重的工作，并且可以立即开始赚取收入。
en: The most economical age to capture an elephant for training is between fifteen and twenty years,for it is then almost ready to undertake heavy work and can begin to earn its keep straight away.

zh: 但这个时代的动物不容易屈服于人类，在早期阶段必须采取非常严厉的手段。
en: But animals of this 15 age do not easily become subservient to man,and a very firm hand must be employed in the early stages.

zh: 被圈养的大象仍然被绳子绑在树上，每当有人靠近时，它就会猛地扑倒并尖叫起来，并且可能会在几天之内因愤怒和恐惧而拒绝所有食物。
en: The captive elephant,still roped to a tree,plunges and screams every time a man approaches,and for several days willprobably refuse all food through anger and fear.

zh: 有时，一头驯服的大象会被拴在附近，以给野生大象带来信心，而在大多数情况下，被俘虏的大象会逐渐安静下来并开始接受食物。
en: Sometimes a tame elephant is tethered nearby to give the wild one confidence,and in most cases the captive gradually quietens down and begins to accept its food.

zh: 下一步是将大象带到训练场所，这是一项棘手的任务，需要用绳子拴在两侧的驯服大象的帮助下才能完成。
en: The next stage is to get the elephant to the training establishment,a ticklish business which is achieved 20 with the aid of two tame elephants roped to the captive on either side.

zh: 理查德·卡林顿大象 第 6 单元第 41 课 在训练的最初几天，一头野生印度象被用绳子拴在两只受过训练的大象身上。
en: RICHARD CARRINGTON Elephants Unit 6 Lesson 41 A wild Indian elephant is roped to two trained ones during the first few days of training.

zh: 地震就像夜间的小偷一样，毫无征兆地来袭。
en: An earthquake comes like a thief in the night,without warning.

zh: 因此，有必要发明一种既不打盹也不睡觉的仪器。
en: It was necessary,therefore,to invent instruments that neither slumbered nor slept.

zh: 有些设备非常简单。
en: Some devices were quite simple.

zh: 例如，其中一种由不同长度和粗细的杆组成，这些杆可以像九瓶柱一样直立起来。
en: One,for instance,consisted of rods of various lengths and thicknesses which would stand up on end like ninepins.

zh: 当震动袭来时，这些东西所在的坚硬桌子都震动了。
en: When a shock came, it shook the rigid table upon which these stood.

zh: 如果动作轻柔，只有更不稳定的杆会掉落。
en: If it were gentle,only the more unstable rods fell.

zh: 如果严重的话，他们都会倒下。
en: If it were severe,they all fell.

zh: 因此，棒通过下落以及下落的方向，为沉睡的科学家记录了太弱而无法唤醒他的冲击的强度，以及冲击的方向。
en: Thus the rods,by falling,and by the direction in which they fell,recorded for the slumbering scientist the strength of a shock that was too weak to waken him,and the direction from which it came.

zh: 但如果要取得任何真正重大的进展，就需要比这更精密的仪器。
en: But instruments far more delicate than that were needed if any really serious advance was to be made.

zh: 我们的理想目标是设计一种仪器，可以用笔在纸上记录地震经过时地面或桌子的运动。
en: The ideal to be aimed at was to devise an instrument that could record with a pen on paper,the movements of the ground or of the table as the quake passed by.

zh: 当我写字时，笔在动，但纸却保持静止。
en: While I write my pen moves,but the paper keeps still.

zh: 毫无疑问，通过练习，我很快就能学会握笔不动而纸动来写字。
en: With practice,no doubt,I could in time learn to write by holding the pen stillwhile the paper moved.

zh: 这听起来是一个愚蠢的建议，但这正是一些早期记录地震波的仪器（地震计）所采用的想法。
en: That sounds a silly suggestion,but that was precisely the idea adopted in some of the early instruments(seismo- meters)for recording earthquake waves.

zh: 但桌子、笔杆、纸都在动，怎么可能写得清楚呢？
en: But when table,penholder and paper are all moving,how is it possible to write legibly?

zh: 解决这个问题的关键在于日常观察。
en: The key to a solution of that problem lay in an everyday observation.

zh: 为什么站在公交车或火车上的人突然启动时容易摔倒？
en: Why does a person standing in a bus or train tend to fall when a sudden start is made?

zh: 这是因为他的脚在前进，但他的头却保持静止。
en: It is because his feet move on,but his head stays still.

zh: 一个简单的实验将帮助我们更进一步。
en: A simple experiment will help us a little further.

zh: 在一根长绳子的末端系上一个重物。
en: Tie a heavy weight at the end of a long piece of string.

zh: 手高举在空中，握住绳子，使重物几乎接触地面。
en: With the hand held high in the air,hold the string so that the weight nearly touches the ground.

zh: 现在来回移动手，但不要上下移动。
en: Now move the hand to and fro and around but not up and down.

zh: 会发现重量移动但轻微或根本没有移动。
en: It will be found that the weight moves but slightiy or not at all.

zh: 想象一下，一支钢笔以这样一种方式连接到重物上，使其笔尖停留在地板上的一张纸上。
en: Imagine a pen attached to the weight in such a way that its point rests upon a piece of paper on the floor.

zh: 想象一下地震震动了地板、纸张、你和你的手。
en: Imagine an earthquake shock shaking the floor,the paper,you and your hand.

zh: 在所有这些运动过程中，重量和笔都是静止的。
en: In the midst of all this movement,the weight and the pen would be still.

zh: 但是，当纸在笔尖下从一侧移动到另一侧时，其移动会被墨水记录在其表面上。
en: But as the paper moved from side to side under the pen point,its movement would be recorded in ink upon its surface.

zh: 正是根据这个原理，第一批乐器被制造出来，但纸被包裹在一个缓慢旋转的鼓上。
en: It was upon this principle that the first instruments were made,but the paper was wrapped round a drum which rotated slowly.

zh: 只要一切静止，笔就画出一条直线，但当鼓摇动时，笔所画的线就会左右蠕动。
en: As long as all was still,the pen drew a straight line,but while the drum was being shaken,the line that the pen was drawing wriggled from side to side.

zh: 然而，这样描述的装置仅记录波浪运动的水平分量，这实际上要复杂得多。
en: The apparatus thus described,however,records only the horizontal component of the wave movement,which is,in fact,much more complicated.

zh: 如果我们真的能看到粒子所描述的路径，比如岩石中的沙粒，它会更像是一只蓝瓶苍蝇在房间里嗡嗡作响；它会上下、来回、从一边到另一边。
en: If we could actually see the path described by a particle,such as a sand grain in the rock,it would be more like that of a bluebottle buzzing round the room;it would be up and down,to and fro and from side to side.

zh: 仪器的设计和放置可以使所有三个元素都可以记录在不同的图表中。
en: Instruments have been devised and can be so placed that all three elements can be recorded in different graphs.

zh: SwINNERTON 我们下面的地球 第 6 单元 第 42 课 伦敦科学博物馆的 Shaw 地震仪 地震仪记录的地震
en: SwINNERTON The Earth beneath Us Unit 6 Lesson 42 The Shaw seismograph at the Science Museum,London An earthquake recorded on a seismograph

zh: 我们必须从那些研究生命起源的人的工作中得出结论，只要有一颗与我们的行星大致相似的行星，生命几乎肯定会开始。
en: We must conclude from the work of those who have studied the origin of life,that given a planet only approximately like our own,life is almost certain to start.

zh: 在我们太阳系的所有行星中，我们现在非常确定地球是唯一一个有生命可以生存的行星。
en: Of all the planets in our own solar system,we are now pretty certain the Earth is the only one on which life can survive.

zh: 火星太干燥且缺氧，金星太热，水星也是如此，外行星的温度接近零，大气层以氢为主。
en: Mars is too dry and poor in oxygen, Venus far too hot,and so is Mercury,and the outer planets have temperatures near absolute zero and hydrogen-dominated atmospheres.

zh: 但其他太阳，即天文学家所说的恒星，必然有像我们自己的行星一样的行星，而且由于宇宙中恒星的数量如此巨大，这种可能性几乎成为必然。
en: But other suns,stars as the astronomers call them,are bound to have planets like our own,and as the number of stars in the universe is so vast,this possibility becomes virtual certainty.

zh: 仅在我们的银河系中就有十亿颗恒星，而宇宙中还有三亿个其他银河系或星系。
en: There are one hundred thousand million stars in our own Milky Way alone,and then there are three thousand million other Milky Ways,or galaxies,in the universe.

zh: 因此，我们目前所知存在的恒星数量估计约为 3 亿颗。
en: So the number of stars that we know exist is now estimated at about 300 million million million.

zh: 虽然也许只有百分之一的生命在某个地方开始发展成为高度复杂和智能的模式，但行星的数量如此之多，智能生命必然是宇宙的自然组成部分。
en: Although perhaps only 1 per cent of the life that has started somewhere will develop into highly complex and intelligent patterns,so vast is the number of planets,that intelligent life is bound to be a natural part of the universe.

zh: 如果我们如此确信宇宙中存在其他智慧生命，为什么我们还没有来自外太空的访客呢？
en: If then we are so certain that other intelligent life exists in the universe,why have we had no visitors from outer space yet?

zh: 首先，他们可能在数千或数百万年前来到了我们这个星球，并发现我们当时盛行的原始状态对他们自己的先进知识完全不感兴趣。
en: First of all,they may have come to this planet of ours thousands or millions of years ago, 15 and found our then prevailing primitive state completely uninteresting to their own advanced knowledge.

zh: 美国著名射电天文学家罗纳德·布雷斯韦尔教授在《自然》杂志上提出，这样一个高级文明在访问我们的太阳系时，可能会留下一个自动信使，等待一个先进文明可能的觉醒。
en: Professor Ronald Bracewell,a leading American radio astronomer,argued in Nature that such a superior civilization,on a visit to our own solar system,may have left an automatic messenger behind to await the possible awakening of an advanced civilization.

zh: 这样的信使，接收到我们的无线电和电视信号，很可能将它们重新传输回其母星，尽管任何其他文明会因此从我们这里得到什么印象最好不要说出来。
en: Such a messenger,receiving our radio and television signals,might well re-transmit them back to its home-planet,although what impression any other 20 civilization would thus get from us is best left unsaid.

zh: 但在这里，我们遇到了与其他星球上的人们接触的最困难的障碍——我们之间的天文距离。
en: But here we come up against the most difficult of all obstacles to contact with people on other planets —the astronomical distances which separate us.

zh: 根据合理的猜测，它们平均距离可能有 100 光年。
en: As a reasonable guess,they might,on an average,be 100 light years away.

zh: （一光年是光在一年内以每秒 186,000 英里的速度传播的距离，即 600 万英里。）无线电波也以光速传播，假设这样一个 25 个自动信使收到了我们 1920 年代的第一次广播，那么发送到其母星的信息就只到了一半。
en: (A light year is the distance which light travels at 186,000 miles per second in one year, namely 6 million million miles.)Radio waves also travel at the speed of light,and assuming such an 25 automatic messenger picked up our first broadcasts of the 1920's,the message to its home planet is barely halfway there.

zh: 同样，我们现有的原始化学火箭虽然足以绕人类运行，但没有机会将我们运送到四光年之外最近的其他恒星，更不用说数十或数百光年的距离了。
en: Similarly,our own present primitive chemical rockets,though good enough to orbit men, have no chance of transporting us to the nearest other star,four light years away,let alone distances of tens or hundreds of light years.

zh: 幸运的是，正如沃尔特·沙利文 30 岁的沃尔特·沙利文在他的优秀著作《我们并不孤单》中所说，我们有一种“独特的理性方式”与其他智慧生物进行交流。
en: Fortunately,there is a 'uniquely rational way'for us to communicate with other intelligent beings,as 30 Walter Sullivan has put it in his excellent book,We Are not Alone.

zh: 这取决于 21 厘米波长的精确射频，即每秒 1420 兆周期。
en: This depends on the precise radio frequency of the 21-cm wavelength,or 1420 megacycles per second.

zh: 它是氢原子在太空中发射的固有频率，由我们于1951年发现；宇宙中任何类型的射电天文学家都必须知道它。
en: It is the natural frequency of emission of the hydrogen atoms in space and was discovered by us in 1951;it must be known to any kind of radio astronomer in the universe.

zh: 一旦这个波长的存在被发现，不久之后就有人建议将其用作星际通信中唯一可识别的 35 个广播频率。
en: Once the existence of this wave-length had been discovered,it was not long before its use as the uniquely 35 recognizable broadcasting frequency for interstellar communication was suggested.

zh: 如果没有第 6 单元第 43 课中的此类内容，在其他星球上寻找智慧生物就像试图在没有预先安排的约会的情况下在伦敦与一位朋友见面一样，并且荒谬地在街道上徘徊，希望有机会相遇。
en: Without something of Unit 6 Lesson 43 this kind,searching for intelligences on other planets would be like trying to meet a friend in London without a pre-aranged rendezvous and absurdly wandering the streets in the hope of a chance encounter.

zh: 安东尼·米凯利斯 太空中有陌生人吗？摘自《周末电讯报》 3 模拟在美国威斯康星州贝尔维尔小镇假设目击 UFO（不明飞行物）。
en: ANTHONY MICHAELIS Are There Strangers in Space?from The Weekend Telegraph 3 Simulation of a hypothetical sighting of a UFO(unidentified flying object) in the small town of Belleville,Wisconsin,USA.

zh: 1986 年至 1989 年的几个月里，该镇一直受到 UFO 目击事件的困扰。
en: For several months in 1986- 1989,the town was plagued by UFO sightings.

zh: 习俗通常不被视为任何伟大时刻的主题。
en: Custom has not commonly been regarded as a subject of any great moment.

zh: 我们觉得我们大脑的内部运作方式特别值得研究，但习惯，我们有一种思维方式，是最常见的行为。
en: The inner workings of our own brains we feel to be uniquely worthy of investigation,but custom,we have a way of thinking,is behaviour at its most commonplace.

zh: 事实上，情况恰恰相反。
en: As a matter of fact,it is the other way around.

zh: 世界各地的传统习俗是一整套详细的行为，比任何一个人在个人行为中所能演化出的东西都要令人惊讶，无论它有多么反常。
en: Traditional custom,taken the world over,is a mass of detailed behaviour more astonishing than what any one person can ever evolve in individual actions,no matter how aberrant.

zh: 但这只是问题的一个相当微不足道的方面。
en: Yet that is a rather trivial aspect of the matter.

zh: 最重要的事实是，习俗在经验和信仰中发挥着主导作用，而且它可能表现出各种各样的变化。
en: The fact off first-rate importance is the predominant role that custom plays in experience and in belief,and the very great varieties it may manifest.

zh: 没有人用纯净的眼睛看世界。
en: No man ever looks at the world with pristine eyes.

zh: 他认为它是由一套明确的习俗、制度和思维方式编辑的。
en: He sees it edited by a definite set of customs and institutions and ways of thinking.

zh: 即使在他的哲学探索中，他也不能落后于这些刻板印象。他对真与假的概念仍然与他特定的传统习俗有关。
en: Even in his philosophical probings he cannot go behind these stereotypes; 10 his very concepts of the true and the false will still have reference to his particular traditional customs.

zh: 约翰·杜威严肃地说，习惯在塑造个人行为方面所起的作用，与他影响传统习惯的任何方式相比，就如同他的母语总词汇量与他自己的儿语词汇中被吸收到他的家庭方言中的词汇的比例一样。
en: John Dewey has said in all seriousness that the part played by custom in shaping the behaviour of the individual, as against any way in which he can affect traditional custom,is as the proportion of the total vocabulary of his mother tongue against those words of his own baby talk that are taken up into the vernacular of his family.

zh: 当人们认真研究有机会自主发展的社会秩序时，这个数字只不过是一种精确而实事求是的观察。
en: When one seriously studies the social orders that have had the opportunity to develop autonomously, 15 the figure becomes no more than an exact and matter-of-fact observation.

zh: 个人的生活史首先是对其社区传统上传承的模式和标准的适应。
en: The life history of the individual is first and foremost an accommodation to the patterns and standards traditionally handed down in his community.

zh: 从他出生的那一刻起，他出生的习俗就塑造了他的经历和行为。
en: From the moment of his birth,the customs into which he is born shape his experience and behaviour.

zh: 当他会说话的时候，他就是他文化中的小生物，当他长大并能够参与其活动时，它的习惯就是他的习惯，它的信仰就是他的信仰，它的不可能就是他的不可能。每个出生在他的群体中的孩子都会与他分享，而地球另一端出生的孩子永远无法达到千分之一。
en: By the time he can talk,he is the little creature of his culture,and by the time he is grown and able to take part in its activities,its habits are his habits,its beliefs his beliefs,its impossibilities his impossibilities. 20 Every child that is born into his group will share them with him,and no child born into one on the opposite side of the globe can ever achieve the thousandth part.

zh: 没有什么社会问题比习俗的作用更值得我们去理解了。
en: There is no social problem it is more incumbent upon us to understand than this of the role of custom.

zh: 在我们了解其规律和多样性之前，人类生活的主要复杂事实必定仍然难以理解。
en: Until we are intelligent as to its laws and varieties,the main complicating facts of human life must remain unintelligible.

zh: 只有在某些初步主张被接受后，对习俗的研究才能有利可图，25并且其中一些主张遭到了强烈反对。
en: The study of custom can be profitable only after certain preliminary propositions have been accepted, 25 and some of these propositions have been violently opposed.

zh: 首先，任何科学研究都要求对其选择考虑的系列中的一个或另一个项目没有优先权重。
en: In the first place,any scientific study requires that there be no preferential weighting of one or another of the items in the series it selects for its consideration.

zh: 在所有争议较小的领域，例如仙人掌或白蚁或星云性质的研究，必要的研究方法是将相关材料分组并记录所有可能的变异形式和条件。
en: In all the less controversial fields,like the study of cacti or termites or the nature of nebulae, the necessary method of study is to group the relevant material and to take note of all possible variant forms and conditions.

zh: 通过这种方式，我们已经了解了所有我们所知道的天文学定律，或者说，30种社会性昆虫的习性。
en: In this way,we have learned all that we know of the laws of astronomy,or of the habits of 30 the social insects,let us say.

zh: 只有在对人本身的研究中，主要的社会科学才取代了对一种局部变异即西方文明的研究。
en: It is only in the study of man himself that the major social sciences have substituted the study of one local variation,that of Western civilization.

zh: 只要我们自己与原始人、我们自己与野蛮人、我们自己与异教徒之间的这些区别在人们的思想中占据主导地位，人类学从定义上来说就是不可能的。
en: Anthropology was by definition impossible,as long as these distinctions between ourselves and the primitive,ourselves and the barbarian,ourselves and the pagan,held sway.over people's minds.

zh: 首先有必要达到一定程度的复杂性，使我们不再以自己的信仰来反对我们第六单元第 44 课邻居的迷信。
en: It was necessary first to arrive at that degree of sophistication where we no longer set our own belief against our Unit 6 Lesson 44 neighbour's superstition.

zh: 有必要认识到，这些基于相同前提的机构，比如说超自然机构，必须一起考虑，我们自己的机构也应该放在一起考虑。
en: It was necessary to recognize that these institutions which are based on the same premises,let us say the supernatural,must be considered together,our own among the rest.

zh: 露丝·本尼迪克特 (RuTH BENEDICT) 文化模式 戴着黄铜项链的缅甸巴东女孩
en: RuTH BENEDICT Patterns of Culture A Padaung girl from Burma in her brass necklace

zh: 在人类早期，与其他生物的竞争一定是至关重要的。
en: In man's early days,competition with other creatures must have been critical.

zh: 但我们的这一阶段的开发现已结束。
en: But this phase of our development is now finished.

zh: 事实上，我们现在缺乏应对原始条件的实践和经验。
en: Indeed,we lack practice and experience nowadays in dealing with primitive conditions.

zh: 我确信，如果没有现代武器，我会在与熊争论洞穴所有权时表现得非常糟糕，在这方面我不认为我是孤立的。
en: I am sure that,without modern weapons,I would make a very poor show of disputing the ownership of a cave with a bear,and in this I do not think that I stand alone.

zh: 最后一个与人类竞争的生物是蚊子。
en: The last creature to compete 5 with man was the mosquito.

zh: 但即使是蚊子也因注意排水和化学喷雾而被抑制住了。
en: But even the mosquito has been subdued by attention to drainage and by chemical sprays.

zh: 然而，我们之间、人与人、社区与社区之间的竞争仍然存在，而且一如既往地激烈。
en: Competition between ourselves,person against person,community against community,still persists, however;and it is as fierce as it ever was.

zh: 但人与人之间的竞争并不是生物学中想象的简单过程。
en: But the competition of man against man is not the simple process envisioned in biology.

zh: 它不是由物理环境决定的固定食物量的简单竞争，因为决定我们进化的环境本质上不再是物理的。
en: It is not a simple 10 competition for a fixed amount of food determined by the physical environment,because the environment that determines our evolution is no longer essentially physical.

zh: 我们的环境主要取决于我们所信仰的事物。
en: Our environment is chiefly conditoned by the things we believe.

zh: 摩洛哥和加利福尼亚是地球上纬度非常相似的两个国家，都位于气候相似的大陆西海岸，并且可能拥有相当相似的自然资源。
en: Morocco and California are bits ofthe Earth in very similar latitudes,both on the west coasts of continents with similar climates,and probably with rather similar natural resources.

zh: 然而他们目前的发展却完全不同，这并不是因为不同的人，而是因为他们的居民心中存在着不同的思想。
en: Yet their present development is wholly different,not so much because of different people even,but because of the 15 different thoughts that exist in the minds of their inhabitants.

zh: 这是我想强调的一点。
en: This is the point I wish to emphasize.

zh: 我们的环境中最重要的因素是我们自己的心态。
en: The most important factor in our environment is the state of our own minds.

zh: 众所周知，白人入侵原始文化时，最具破坏性的影响不是来自物质武器，而是来自思想。
en: It is well known that where the white man has invaded a primitive culture,the most destructive effects have come not from physical weapons but from ideas.

zh: 想法是危险的。
en: Ideas are dangerous.

zh: 神圣办公室在过去导致异端分子被烧死的时候就清楚地知道这一点。
en: The Holy Office knew this full well when it caused heretics to be burned in days gone by.

zh: 事实上，言论自由的概念只存在于我们的现代社会中，因为当你在一个社区内时，你受到社区惯例的制约，以至于很难想象任何真正具有破坏性的事情。
en: Indeed,the concept of free speech only exists in 20 our modern society because when you are inside a community,you are conditioned by the conventions of the community to such a degree that it is very difficult to conceive of anything really destructive.

zh: 只有旁观者才能注入危险的想法。
en: It is only someone looking on from outside that can inject the dangerous thoughts.

zh: 我毫不怀疑，向现代世界注入会彻底摧毁我们的思想是可能的。
en: I do not doubt that it would be possible to inject ideas into the modern world that would utterly destroy us.

zh: 我想给你举个例子，但幸运的是我不能这样做。
en: I would like to give you an example,but fortunately I cannot do so.

zh: 或许只要提及核弹就足够了。
en: Perhaps it willsuffice to mention the nuclear bomb.

zh: 想象一下 25 对一个相当先进的技术社会的影响，一个仍然没有炸弹的社会，让它意识到可能性，提供足够的细节来建造这个东西。
en: Imagine the 25 effect on a reasonably advanced technological society,one that still does not possess the bomb,of making it aware of the possibility,of supplying sufficient details to enable the thing to be constructed.

zh: 1925 年左右交给任何一个世界大国的二十或三十页信息就足以改变世界历史的进程。
en: Twenty or thirty pages of information handed to any of the major world powers around the year 1925 would have been sufficient to change the course of world history.

zh: 这是一个奇怪的想法，但我相信这是一个正确的想法，二十或三十页的想法和信息将能够使当今世界发生三十个翻天覆地的变化，甚至摧毁它。
en: It is a strange thought,but I believe a correct one,that twenty or thirty pages of ideas and information would be capable of turning the present-day world upside 30 down,or even destroying it.

zh: 我经常尝试设想这些页面可能包含什么内容，但我当然不能这样做，因为我是当今世界的囚犯，就像你们所有人一样。
en: I have often tried to conceive of what those pages might contain,but of course I cannot do so because I am a prisoner of the present-day world,just as all of you are.

zh: 我们不能在我们的大脑所适应的特定模式之外思考，或者，更准确地说，我们只能在外面思考一点点，而且只有当我们非常有创意时。
en: We cannot think outside the particular patterns that our brains are conditioned to,or,to be more accurate,we can think only a very little way outside,and then only if we are very original.

zh: FRED HoYLE 《人类与星系》第 6 单元第 45 课 加利福尼亚州索萨利托的鸟瞰图
en: FRED HoYLE Of Men and Galaxies Unit 6 Lesson 45 An aerial view of Sausalito,California

zh: 一位才华横溢的美国心理学家曾说过：“忧虑是一种情绪的痉挛；头脑抓住某样东西，不肯放手。”
en: A gifted American psychologist has said,'Worry is a spasm of the emotion;the mind catches hold of something and will not let it go.

zh: 在这种情况下与头脑争论是没有用的。
en: 'It is useless to argue with the mind in this condition.

zh: 意志越坚强，任务就越徒劳。
en: The stronger the will, the more futile the task.

zh: 一个人只能轻轻地将其他东西引入它的痉挛之中。
en: One can only gently insinuate something else into its convulsive grasp.

zh: 如果正确地选择了其他的东西，如果它确实伴随着另一个感兴趣领域的启发，逐渐地，而且往往很快，旧的不适当的束缚就会放松，恢复和修复的过程就会开始。
en: And if this something else is rightly chosen,if it is really attended by the illumination of another field of interest, 5 gradually,and often quite swiftly,the old undue grip relaxes and the process of recuperation and repair begins.

zh: 因此，培养一种爱好和新的兴趣形式对于一个公众人物来说是最重要的政策。
en: The cultivation of a hobby and new forms of interest is therefore a policy of the first importance to a public man.

zh: 但这不是一天就能完成的事情，也不是仅仅靠意志就能迅速完成的事情。
en: But this is not a business that can be undertaken in a day or swiftly improvised by a mere command of the will.

zh: 另类心理兴趣的成长是一个漫长的过程。
en: The growth of alternative mental interests is a long process.

zh: 种子必须精心挑选；它们必须落在好土里；它们必须精心照料，这样才能在需要时结出充满活力的果实。
en: The seeds must be carefully 10 chosen;they must fallon good ground;they must be sedulously tended,if the vivifying fruits are to be at hand when needed.

zh: 要想真正快乐、真正安全，一个人至少应该有两到三种爱好，而且它们都必须是真实的。
en: To be really happy and really safe,one ought to have at least two or three hobbies,and they must all be real.

zh: 在晚年才说：我会对这个或那个感兴趣，这是没有用的。
en: It is no use starting late in life to say:I will take an interest in this or that.

zh: “这样的尝试只会加剧脑力劳动的压力。
en: 'Such an attempt only aggravates the strain of mental effort.

zh: 一个人可能会获得与日常工作无关的大量知识，但几乎得不到任何好处或缓解。
en: A man may acquire great knowledge of topics unconnected with his 15 daily work,and yet get hardly any benefit or relief.

zh: 做你喜欢做的事是没有用的；你必须喜欢你所做的事。
en: It is no use doing what you like;you have got to like what you do.

zh: 从广义上讲，人可以分为三类：劳累死的人、忧虑死的人、无聊死的人。
en: Broadly speaking,human beings may be divided into three classes:those who are toiled to death, those who are worried to death,and those who are bored to death.

zh: 对于体力劳动者来说，一周辛苦的汗水和努力已经让他们筋疲力尽，周六下午给他们提供踢足球或棒球的机会是没有用的。
en: It is no use offering the manual labourer, tired out with a hard week's sweat and effort,the chance of playing a game of football or baseball on Saturday afternoon.

zh: 邀请已经工作或担心了六天严肃事情的政治家、专业人士或商人在周末工作或担心琐事是没有用的。
en: It is no use inviting the politician or the professional or business man,who has been working or worrying about serious things for six days,to work or worry about trifling things at the weekend.

zh: 至于那些可以掌控自己想要的一切、可以满足一切任性、几乎可以得到所有欲望对象的不幸的人——对他们来说，新的快乐、新的兴奋只是一种额外的满足。
en: As for the unfortunate people who can command everything they want,who can gratify every caprice and lay their hands on almost every object of desire—for them a new pleasure,a new excitement is only an additional satiation.

zh: 他们徒劳地疯狂地从一个地方跑到另一个地方，试图通过仅仅敲击声和运动来摆脱 25 的无聊。
en: In vain they rush frantically round from place to place,trying to escape from avenging 25 boredom by mere clatter and motion.

zh: 对他们来说，某种形式的纪律是最有希望的道路。
en: For them discipline in one form or another is the most hopeful path.

zh: 也可以说，理性、勤奋、有用的人可以分为两类：第一类，工作就是工作，快乐就是快乐；第二类，工作与快乐合而为一。
en: It may also be said that rational,industrious,useful human beings are divided into two classes:first, those whose work is work and whose pleasure is pleasure;and secondly,those whose work and pleasure are one.

zh: 其中，前者占大多数。
en: Of these the former are the majority.

zh: 他们有他们的补偿。
en: They have their compensations.

zh: 在办公室或工厂里长时间工作给他们带来的回报不仅是维持生计，而且是对快乐的强烈渴望 30 ，即使是最简单和最谦虚的形式。
en: The long hours in the office or the factory bring with them as their reward,not only the means of sustenance,but a keen appetite for pleasure 30 even in its simplest and most modest forms.

zh: 但命运眷顾的孩子却属于第二等。
en: But Fortune's favoured children belong to the second class.

zh: 他们的生活是自然和谐的。
en: Their life is a natural harmony.

zh: 对他们来说，工作时间永远不够长。
en: For them the working hours are never long enough.

zh: 每天都是假期，而普通的假期一旦到来，就会被视为强行打断令人着迷的职业而感到不情愿。
en: Each day is a holiday, and ordinary holidays,when they come,are grudged as enforced interruptions in an absorbing vocation.

zh: 然而，对于这两个班级来说，需要另一种观点、改变气氛、转移精力，这是第 6 单元第 46 课的本质。
en: Yet to both classes,the need of an alternative outlook,of a change of atmosphere,of a diversion of effort,is Unit 6 Lesson 46 essentia.

zh: 事实上，那些以工作为乐趣的人很可能最需要采取措施35将工作从他们的脑海中消除。
en: Inded,it may well be that those whose workis their pleasure are those who most need the means 35 of banishing it at intervals from their minds.

zh: 温斯顿·丘吉尔 (WINSTON CHURCHIL) 绘画作为一种消遣 温斯顿·丘吉尔爵士 1946 年在瑞士作画
en: WINSTON CHURCHIL Painting as a Pastime Sir Winston Churchill painting in Switzerland in 1946

zh: 经济是露营的一个强大动机，因为在设备的初始费用或通过租赁之后，总费用可以远远低于酒店的费用。
en: Economy is one powerful motive for camping,since after the initial outlay upon equipment,or through hiring it,the total expense can be far less than the cost of hotels.

zh: 但是，与流行的假设相反，它远不是唯一的，甚至不是最伟大的。
en: But,contrary to a popular assumption,it is far from being the only one,or even the greatest.

zh: 一个人如果不小心进入欧洲无数永久地点之一的二十磅重的空间，可能会发现自己撞上了一辆宾利车。
en: The man who manoeuvres carelessly into his twenty pounds' worth of space at one of Europe's myriad permanent sites may find himself bumping a Bentley.

zh: 更有可能的是，福特护航将与雷诺或梅赛德斯建立枢纽对枢纽，但很少与两人合作的自行车。
en: More likely, 5 Ford Escort will be hub to hub with Renault or Mercedes,but rarely with bicycles made for two.

zh: 现代露营的设备每年都变得更加先进，这对于愤世嫉俗的人来说是一个有趣的悖论，对于那些发誓要远离这一切的满怀希望的旅行者来说，这是一个更光明的希望。
en: That the equipment of modern camping becomes yearly more sophisticated is an entertaining paradox for the cynic,a brighter promise for the hopeful traveller who has sworn to get away from it all.

zh: 它还提供了另一种逃避方式——一些学生社会学家可能愿意将他的论文建立在这种现象的基础上。
en: It also provides—and some student sociologist might care to base his thesis upon the phenomenon—an escape of another kind.

zh: 现代旅行者常常不喜欢辉煌和贝拉维斯塔，不是因为他买不起或回避它们的物质享受，而是因为他害怕它们。
en: The modern traveller is often a man who dislikes the Splendide and the Bellavista,not because 10 he cannot afford,or shuns their material comforts,but because he is afraid of them.

zh: 他可能很富有，但他决不知道该给门童或女服务员多少小费。
en: Affluent he may be,but he is by no means sure what to tip the doorman or the chambermaid.

zh: 作为自己家里的主人，他不知道什么时候该对酒店领班说“嘘”。
en: Master in his own house,he has little idea of when to say boo to a maitre d'hôtel.

zh: 露营让他摆脱了所有这些恐惧。
en: From all such fears camping releases him.

zh: 诚然，基于装备和技术的露营本身的势利已经存在；但它是一种，如果他遇到它，他可以很容易地理解和处理。管理层和酒店等级制度中没有高级的“他们”让他的假期变得黑暗。
en: Granted,a snobbery of camping itself,based upon equipment and techniques,already exists;but it is of a kind that,if he meets it,he can readily understand and deal with. 15 There is no superior 'they'in the shape of managements and hotel hierarchies to darken his holiday days.

zh: 除了这样的动机之外，还必须加上另一个动机。
en: To such motives,yet another must be added.

zh: 当代汽车崇拜现象的一个重要原因是拥有汽车所带来的独立和自由感。
en: The contemporary phenomenon of car worship is to be explained not least by the sense of independence and freedom that ownershipentails.

zh: 露营给这种乐趣带来了精致的享受。
en: To this pleasure camping gives an exquisite refinement.

zh: 从自己的家门到家乡或外国的山丘或沙地再回来，一切都在手边。
en: From one's own front door to home or foreign hills or sands and back again,everything is to hand.

zh: 不仅到达度假天堂的方法完全在自己的指挥和掌控之内，而且逃离假日地狱的方法（如果海滩太拥挤，当地的天气太恶劣）就在那里，在帐篷外面——或者很可能是帐篷的一部分。
en: Not 20 only are the means of arriving at the holiday paradise entirely within one's own command and keeping,but the means of escape from holiday hell(if the beach proves too crowded,the local weather too inclement)are there,outside—or,as likely,part of—the tent.

zh: 理想主义者反对露营的做法，正如旅行团一样，出国旅游的人因此而失去了了解所到国家人民的机会。
en: Idealists have objected to the practice of camping,as to the package tour,that the traveller abroad thereby denies himself the opportunity of getting to know the people of the country visited.

zh: 有人认为，孤立性和自我封闭是相辅相成的。
en: Insularity and self-containment,it is argued,go hand in hand.

zh: 这种观点并不适用于流行的大陆露营地的体验。
en: The opinion does not survive experience of a popular Continental camping place.

zh: 度假酒店往往专门为某一国籍的游客提供服务，有时甚至专门针对某一国籍的游客。
en: Holiday hotels tend to cater for one nationality of visitors especially,sometimes exclusively.

zh: 相比之下，露营地则高度国际化。
en: Camping sites,by contrast,are highly cosmopolitan.

zh: 诚然，德国人占多数是大多数地中海地区的共同特征；但迄今为止还没有压倒性的专业赞助。
en: Granted,a preponderance of Germans is a characteristic that seems common to most Mediterranean sites;but as yet there is no overwhelmingly specialized patronage.

zh: 禁止露天晾晒衣服、禁止使用水点洗车、禁止邀请“我们的露营朋友”参加舞会或乘船游览的通知不仅用法语、意大利语或西班牙语印制，而且还用英语、德语和荷兰语印制。
en: Notices forbidding the open-air drying of clothes,or the use of water points for car 30 washing,or those inviting ‘our camping friends'to a dance or a boat trip are printed not only in French or Italian or Spanish,but also in English,German and Dutch.

zh: 吃饭的时候，酸菜的气味和大蒜的气味相互竞争。
en: At meal times the odour of sauerkraut vies with that of garlic.

zh: 法国人的早餐咖啡与英国人的培根和鸡蛋竞争。
en: The Frenchman's breakfast coffee competes with the Englishman's bacón and eggs.

zh: 很难说有组织的露营活动的显着增长是否意味着更独立的露营活动的最终消亡。
en: Whether the remarkable growth of organized camping means the eventual death of the more independent kind is hard to say.

zh: 市政当局自然希望确保露营者的场地费和其他习俗。
en: Municipalities naturally want to secure the campers'site fees and other custom.

zh: 警察 35 对无法追踪到公认的营地边界或四堵墙的流动人员保持警惕。
en: Police 35 are wary of itinerants who cannot be traced to a recognized camp boundary or to four walls.

zh: 但大多数第 6 单元第 47 课可能完全取决于露营者自己：他们引起了多少荒野火灾；他们留下了多少垃圾；简而言之，他们是否完全疏远了土地所有者和住在乡村的人们。
en: But most Unit 6 Lesson 47 probably it will all depend upon campers themselves:how many heath fires they cause;how much litter they leave;in short,whether or not they wholly alienate landowners and those who live in the countryside.

zh: 只有良好的童子军才能维护永恒童子军心中所珍视的自由。
en: Only good scouting is likely to preserve the freedoms so dear to the heart of the eternal Boy Scout.

zh: 奈杰尔·巴克斯顿 (NIGEL BuXTON) 周末大逃亡 电讯报 现代露营
en: NIGEL BuXTON The Great Escape from The Weekend Telegraph Modern camping

zh: 不乏提供“快速致富”机会的消息人士。
en: There is no shortage of tipsters around offering ‘get-rich-quick’opportunities.

zh: 但如果您是一位认真的私人投资者，请将拉斯维加斯心态留给那些有钱可以挥霍的人。
en: But if you are a serious private investor,leave the Las Vegas mentality to those with money to fritter.

zh: 认真的投资者需要一个合适的“投资组合”——精心策划的投资选择，具有明确的结构和明确的目标。
en: The serious investor needs a proper ‘portfolio'—a well-planned selection of investments,with a definite structure and a clear aim.

zh: 但股市新手究竟如何实现这一目标呢？
en: But exactly how does a newcomer to the stock market go about achieving that?

zh: 好吧，如果你去找五位信誉良好的股票经纪人，问他们你应该用你的钱做什么，你很可能会得到五种不同的答案——即使你提供了有关你的年龄、家庭、财务状况以及你想从投资中得到什么的所有相关信息。
en: Well,if you go to five reputable stock brokers and ask them what you should do with your money,you're likely to get five different answers,—even if you give all the relevant information about your age,family, finances and what you want from your investments.

zh: 道德？
en: Moral?

zh: 构建投资组合并不存在一种“正确”的方法。
en: There is no one 'right'way to structure a portfolio.

zh: 然而，毫无疑问，存在一些错误的方法，并且您可以确定，我们的五位顾问中没有一个会建议将您的所有（或可能任何）资金投入 Periwigs*。
en: However,there are undoubtedly some wrong ways,and you can be sure that none of our five advisers would have suggested sinking all(or perhaps any)of your money into Periwigs*.

zh: 那么你应该做什么呢？
en: So what should you do?

zh: 我们假设您已经解决了基本问题，例如抵押贷款、养老金、保险和获得足够的现金储备。
en: We'll assume that you have sorted out the basics—like mortgages,pensions, insurance and access to sufficient cash reserves.

zh: 然后你应该建立自己的个人目标。
en: You should then establish your own individual aims.

zh: 这些部分是个人情况的问题，部分是心理的问题。
en: These are partly a matter of personal circumstances,partly a matter of psychology.

zh: 例如，如果您年龄较大，您从重大损失中恢复过来的时间就会减少，您可能希望增加养老金收入。
en: For instance,if you are older you have less time to recover from any major losses,and you may well wish to boost your pension income.

zh: 因此，保存您的资本并创造额外收入是您的首要任务。
en: So preserving your capital and generating extra income are your main priorities.

zh: 在这种情况下，您可能会构建一个包含一些股票（但不是高风险股票）的投资组合，以及英国国债、现金存款，也许还有可转换债券或分割资本投资信托的收入份额。
en: In this case,you'd probably construct a portfolio with some shares(but not high risk ones),along with gilts,cash deposits,and perhaps convertibles or the income shares of split capital investment trusts.

zh: 如果你比较年轻，财务状况稳健，你可能会决定采取激进的做法——但前提是你有乐观的性格，不会因为股价而彻夜难眠。
en: If you are younger,and in a solid financial position,you may decide to take an aggressive approach- but only if you're blessed with a sanguine disposition and won't suffer sleepless nights over share prices.

zh: 如果您在这个描述中认出了自己，那么您的投资组合中可能会包括一些令人兴奋的成长型股票，以及您的平淡投资。
en: If you recognize yourself in this description,you might include a couple of heady growth stocks in your portfolio,alongside your more pedestrian investments.

zh: 一旦确定了投资目标，您就可以决定将资金投向何处。
en: Once you have decided on your investment aims, you can then decide where to put your money.

zh: 这里的黄金法则是分散你的风险——如果你把所有的钱都投入 Periwigs International，那么你就让自己成为了财富的人质。 *“Periwigs”是一家虚构公司的名称。
en: The golden rule here is spread your risk—if you put all of your money into Periwigs International,you're setting yourself up as a hostage to fortune. *‘Periwigs'is the name of a fictitious company.

zh: 投资者纪事，231990 年 3 月
en: INVESTOR's CHRONICLE,March 231990
